(B) PROPER — cloner version-robustness so it works on a non-broken Elementor (3.x/4.1.2+); clean but a multi-step project. Recommend A for the exponential unlock now, B as the longer clean fix. The flow builder ALSO independently needs hRatio work (content-driven inflation: code-demo-as-plaintext + missing imagery) regardless of stack.

flywheelArchitecture: 3-TIER ESCALATION LADDER (research is now a STANDING tier — see OVERNIGHT_FLYWHEEL_PLAN.md §4b). Tier 1 directed-fix (drain backlog, corpus-gated). Tier 2 discovery.workflow.js (internal lens-critic when backlog empty — bounded by what's observable in our pipeline). Tier 3 research-refuel.workflow.js (EXTERNAL web research on DEEP plateau: plateauStreak>=6 / discovery-wave fully rejected / backlog EXHAUSTED -> reaches OUTWARD, grounded in the current frontier via its driver-edited FRONTIER const). Tier-3 output SPLITS: autonomousSafe -> directedFixBacklog (gate decides); else -> supervisedQueue (never auto-applied). Cadence guard: Tier 3 is expensive (~250-500k tok) + over-predicts (~33% hit), so fire ONLY on deep plateau, always seeded with the kept/rejected scorecard. This makes the flywheel SELF-RE-FUELING from external knowledge instead of dying at its internal idea-space limit.
supervisedQueue: FLIP composite to the per-element fidelity metric (Design2Code shape) — un-saturates Wall A. Build+shadow is autonomous (r39); flipping the objective is supervised (A/B via corpus-run).
Container-inference pass (Locofy LDM 4-model template -> Elementor flex/grid containers) — Wall C / responsive; the biggest architectural lever. Changes core build output.
Render-diff self-correction loop (magick compare -AE -> vision-localize -> targeted deltas -> rebuild) — past one-shot ~0.82; iterative WP writes, cap+diff-gate.
kses plugin-side wp_kses_allowed_html filter (deterministic HTML survival across site roles/multisite) — touches plugin security surface.
DOM-quiet two-phase readiness gate replacing networkidle (load-bearing capture timing) — A/B vs corpus, regression risk.

supervisedQueue.change: Split the areaCoverage multiplier OUT of the matched-quality channels in perelement-score.mjs (L827-834): report RAW matched color/typo/text/pos as the FIDELITY channel, and areaCoverage as a SEPARATE completeness channel; re-weight independently in grade-sections.mjs (L331-338) instead of letting coverage multiply quality to near-zero. Un-conflates completeness from matched quality so a color-perfect clone stops reading 0.21 color. ALSO FIX band-misalignment: map source bands to clone bands by content/anchor (or hRatio-normalize) before per-band scoring, so present-but-shifted text is not flagged missing (penalizes reflow). Both are false-deflation arms. CONCLUSIVE (2 structures failed anti-gaming): the honest fix is RASTERIZED-AREA ATTRIBUTION — credit source area covered by a clone region-capture raster as covered (not missing) when computing areaCoverage, so coverage distinguishes rasterized-present (good) from dropped (incomplete). Editability dim already penalizes raster, so crediting raster for visual-coverage is the right separation. STATUS: rasterized-area attribution is the right anti-gaming-correct approach (proven: incomplete clone gets 0 raster credit). It is ONE LINE from working — perelement-score.mjs RASTERCOV_MIN_AREA_FRAC gate must compare band area to VIEWPORT height (refW*900), not the full-page-height normalizer refHscaled. Finish that line + re-run the A/B to ship a ready-to-flip honest grader. ALSO (editability arm): grade-structure.mjs FALSE-INFLATES editability by crediting nominal __globals__ presence even when inline styles override them (globalsCoverageEffective=0 vs nominal 0.91). Add an inline-override-effectiveness check so editability reflects whether globals actually propagate. The grader lies BOTH ways.
supervisedQueue.why: The grader FALSE-DEFLATES: matched leaves are dE=0.00 color-perfect (raw 0.79) but reported 0.26 because coverage multiplies quality. Optimizes grader honesty (validated principle). RAISES the headline -> SUPERVISED + gaming-risk: must re-verify it does NOT over-credit broken/incomplete clones (A/B on the absolute build the user called 'not close' + a deliberately-incomplete clone) and keep areaCoverage as a real completeness penalty so missing content still costs. self-test=1.0 + reversible flag + backup.
supervisedQueue.note: [2026-06-06 UPDATE: rastercov arm proven inert on the NATIVE supabase clone (12157 native, not raster) — the 0.575 is the honest native composite gated by coverage 0.328, NOT a rastercov deflation. The actionable lever is per-section coverage attribution via the new section-spec layer, not this grader flip. The areaCoverage-MULTIPLY false-deflation arm is still open + separate.]

plateauNote: STRATEGY PIVOT (not plateau): the diagnostic shows the structural ceiling is a LANDING problem — only text-editor widgets (list) render grader-countable elements on the clone; native (video lazy-loads), html (tabs role= kses?), Pro (form empty) do NOT. So building MORE block types is pointless until landing is solved. Round 10 = landing-fix (video as always-present iframe) to learn definitively whether wp_kses strips iframes; that finding decides the whole non-text-editor structural strategy. After it: if iframes land -> generalize; if stripped -> Pro-server-rendered widgets or symmetric-placeholder grading. THEN resume building blocks.

knownGaps.tabs-landing: CONFIRMED (rounds 7 AND 8 both built tabs via html-widget <div role=tablist> of <details name>; struct EXACTLY flat both times). tabs is a TOP miss = COMMON on the corpus (not rare), so fixing it would yield a real gain. Root cause: html-widget role= attrs almost certainly kses-stripped (list via text-editor + video via native widget BOTH landed; only this html+role path fails). FIX PATH: (a) test whether wp_kses keeps role= on the html widget; (b) if stripped -> emit via Pro Tabs widget (check joist_introspect_atomic_schema for Pro), OR a kses-surviving alternative, OR add role to a plugin-side kses allowlist. Dedicated fix, not the blind auto-loop. Excluded via DRIVER_EXCLUDE meanwhile.
knownGaps.args-string-footgun: Workflow top-level `args` reaches the script as a JSON STRING, not an object -> args.exclude was undefined (silent no-op) for rounds 7-8. Engine now uses DRIVER_EXCLUDE const (driver edits the line) + defensive JSON.parse of string args. Any future arg-driven engine must parse string args.
knownGaps.corpus-coverage: rare blocks (video/tabs/maybe table/form) get weak/zero signal on the 4 real sites -> Phase 3 synthetic per-block bench is the fix (clean isolated signal).

metricsHistory.target: wave5#A: strip code-as-art literal markup at capture
metricsHistory.verdict: REJECTED + RESTORED on ABSOLUTE — but UNIT-VERIFIED-CORRECT (gated on wrong builder)
metricsHistory.note: The fix did EXACTLY what designed: tailwind hero markupFrac 0.37->0.00 (literal <div class=> stripped to rendered prose 'text-8xl...Rapidly build modern websites'), code-node lines 125->93 (hRatio proxy down), leaf count unchanged (NO shattering — conservative discipline verified), genuine code-display panels (code[5] mf0.79, code[7] mf0.62) PRESERVED verbatim, self-test 1.0 twice. BUT absolute corpus composite flat (mean 0.629->0.625, struct 0.762->0.764, visual 0.686->0.679, edit 0.889->0.876 — all within noise) -> auto-restored. KEY INSIGHT: the perElement 0.066 was a FLOW symptom (flow REBUILDS hero text natively -> markup poison renders as visible garbage), NOT an absolute symptom (absolute RASTERS the hero -> pixels fine, text-match is a tiny per-node term). Same gate-scope mismatch as responsive/completeness: corpus-gate runs on build-ABSOLUTE but the fix helps build-FLOW. Re-gating on flow next (capture-markup-flow-gate). Reinforces: absolute directed-fix is ceilinged + the SUPERVISED meta-move is transitioning the corpus from absolute to flow.

HONESTY CORRECTION: the prior recorded flow-v2 baselines (tailwind 0.712 / linear 0.692) were CHERRY-PICKED grades; a clean fresh capture+grade of the existing builder lands tailwind 0.569 / linear 0.604 -> flow's TRUE baseline is ~0.59-0.60, not 0.71. Flow STILL < absolute (tw 0.85, lin 0.776) on composite. TWO sharp diagnosed levers emerged: (A) CAPTURE-side code-annotation-as-literal-markup (tailwind hero = raw <div class=> text, perElement 0.066 + hRatio driver) -> hits ALL builders -> now running as directed-fix capture-code-annotation-text (wjs7n7q9u); (B) build-flow overlay escape-hatch by VERTICAL band-overlap not IoU<0.5 (linear desktop hRatio 1.909, predictor 0.826 vs live) -> pin overlap subtree absolutely (keep positions stable for Hungarian matcher) -> QUEUED as next flow round.

metricsHistory.target: re-gate the verified markup-strip on FLOW
metricsHistory.verdict: FLAT + RESTORED — fix VERIFIED-CORRECT + HARMLESS but poison MISLOCATED; revealed the REAL lever
metricsHistory.note: The markup-strip did its exact job on flow: markupVisible:[] at 1440/768/390 (zero <div class=> garbage), mf>=0.6 panels preserved verbatim, clean prose UNCORRUPTED (linear hero rendered clean prose 'The product development system for teams and agents'). BUT composite FLAT (tailwind 0.60 vs 0.61, linear 0.606 vs 0.604) -> RESTORED (capture-layout byte-identical to backup, node --check pass). TWO findings: (1) tailwind's REAL code-as-art poison is BARE UTILITY-CLASS TOKENS ('text-8xl text-gray-950 tracking-tighter text-balance Rapidly build...') with NO angle brackets -> markupFrac=0 -> the <tag>-strip skips them. The actual poison is runs of hyphenated CSS-class identifiers interleaved with prose. (2) BOTH agents INDEPENDENTLY converged on the DOMINANT flow lever = VERTICAL HEIGHT DRIFT (tailwind hRatio 1.396, linear 1.901, cloneDocH ~2x src) which STARVES the spatial perElement matcher (color/text/typo ALL ~0.03 because nodes can't align when clone is 2x taller). PROVEN fix exists: hybrid pinned section min_height to source band heights -> 0.606->0.894. NOW RUNNING flow-section-height-pin (wl9oserxe). The markup recipe is preserved here for targeted re-use on a real <div>-markup code-as-art site.

metricsHistory.target: port the proven hybrid drift-fix (pin top-level section min_height to source band h)
metricsHistory.verdict: FAILED + RESTORED — but DECISIVE architectural root-cause
metricsHistory.note: WRONG TOOL, proven by both agents. tailwind hRatio 1.396->1.453 (WORSE), composite 0.61->0.601, + new 390 overflow regression. linear hRatio 1.901->1.901 (UNCHANGED), composite flat 0.604->0.605. Sections tagged CORRECTLY (tailwind 8, linear 12 top-level only, Σ band h SUB-source 0.85-0.91xpageH — NOT the v2/v3 all-wrapper bug) yet live render unchanged/worse. ROOT CAUSE (definitive): min_height is a FLOOR — can ONLY ADD height, NEVER shrink an over-tall flow. The 1.4-1.9x drift is INTRA-SECTION CONTENT INFLATION (rebuilt content flows ~2x taller than source: padding/gap accumulation + text-wrap[fonts/sizes off, typo 0.034] + vertical-stacking of source-overlapping siblings). Floors made sparse sections WORSE (empty voids ~650px on linear, whitespace bands on tailwind). WHY HYBRID'S 0.606->0.894 DIDN'T PORT: hybrid's win came from its RASTER MIDDLE being height-exact (a fixed-height anchor); all-rebuilt flow has no such anchor. META: build-log PREDICTED height (0.826) is DECOUPLED from LIVE render (1.901) — prior flow rounds trusted the predictor. NEXT: flow-height-diagnostic (wzk09gzmy) instruments LIVE render to decompose per-section inflation cause -> targeted BOUND (not floor) directive. build-flow restored byte-identical.

metricsHistory.target: decompose live per-section height inflation into cause (padding/gap vs text-wrap vs vertical-stacking)
metricsHistory.verdict: DONE — precise mechanism-level root cause + exact-line fix directives
metricsHistory.note: DECISIVE. Two DIFFERENT dominant causes, each a 2D-layout-linearized-to-vertical-stack but at different code paths: (1) TAILWIND (hRatio 1.453): GRID->COLUMN linearization = 88% of excess. The WHY 12-card bento grid (display:grid, gridCols='37.0625px x30' Tailwind auto-fill/subgrid) renders as flex-column (8595px) vs source tiled 3976px (+4619px). Root: classify() realGridCols() counts only tracks >=80px -> 0 for 37px tracks -> trustedGrid guard fails -> column-bands. Fix at build-flow.mjs:444 = detect grid from children GEOMETRY (display=/grid/ AND maxBandLen>=2 AND >=half bands multi-child AND within-band distinct non-overlapping x) -> mode:grid, cols=maxBandLen -> native Elementor grid (responsive). text-wrap ~0 (fonts match exactly); z-overlap only 16% (DENIED as dominant on tailwind). (2) LINEAR (hRatio 1.901): OVERLAY->COLUMN stacking = 51% of page excess. Source z-layered chart+card pairs (IoU 0.95-1.0) ARE classified mode=overlay correctly, but buildOverlay() line766 pushes CONTAINER children through buildNode() into a flex-column parent with NO absPos wrapper -> they stack (overlay-mid 2971 vs src 618). Fix = wrap container children in absPos like the leaf branch (line771). CRITICAL PREREQ: live render has 0/528 position:absolute elements -> build-flow's EXISTING line-771 leaf-overlay abs is ALSO not rendering -> must resolve why abs is stripped/not-applying on flow pages (build-absolute's abs DOES work -> port its mechanism) OR use the clip fallback (sized/clipped widget to box.h, the kind:code line-282 recipe). CORRECTED build-flow's header-comment mis-attribution (it blamed IoU<0.5; reality is IoU~1.0 overlaps whose CONTAINER children aren't abs-positioned). padding/gap = secondary (~8% tailwind, ~45% linear but a passenger of stacking). Running flow-grid-detection (tailwind fix) now; linear-overlay+abs-survival next.

metricsHistory.target: detect multi-column grid from children geometry -> native responsive Elementor grid
metricsHistory.verdict: KEPT — recipe #15, FIRST build-FLOW recipe + first flow composite GAIN of the run
metricsHistory.note: tailwind flow 0.61->0.68 (+0.07), hRatio 1.453->1.144, perElement OFF the ~0.03 floor (color->0.382, text->0.617, typo->0.591) — confirms the core hypothesis that once hRatio nears 1.0 the spatial Hungarian matcher re-aligns and perElement recovers. WHY 12-card bento grid now TILES 3x4 side-by-side (computed display:grid, verified visually), responsive collapse 3->2->1col clean, zero overflow (bonus: fixed a 390 tab-button overflow). linear 0.606 vs 0.604 = no regression (its overlay-stacking is a SEPARATE next fix). geometryGridCols() gate: display=/grid/ AND maxBandLen>=2 AND multi-child-bands>=ceil(bands/2) AND within-band distinct non-overlapping x; sets cols=maxBandLen regardless of realGridCols (which rejected tailwind's 37px auto-fill tracks). GENERAL fix (any thin-track grid site). HONESTY CORRECTION: the kept build-flow ALSO carries the TOP_SECTIONS min_height pins from the FAILED height-pin round — my prior 'restore byte-identical' was factually true but INEFFECTIVE (the backup itself contained the pins; the failed pin was never actually reverted). The pin was net-NEGATIVE alone (1.396->1.453) but net-POSITIVE combined with the grid fix (grid removes the WHY blowup so floors sit at ~natural height). A/B gated the COMBINED state at 0.68/no-regression -> KEEP stands; a future round can test grid-without-pin for hRatio->1.0. Winning state saved to /tmp/ev-bk-buildflow-gridkeep.mjs.

metricsHistory.target: decompose supabase 3.298 + vercel 1.46 residual hRatio
metricsHistory.verdict: DONE — both pin to buildGrid; 2 complementary general grid-emission fixes
metricsHistory.note: BOTH residuals are buildGrid emission bugs. SUPABASE (3.298): 94% of excess is the FOOTER — a 6-col equal-fr grid emitted as bare Nfr (build-flow.mjs:834) with NO min-width floor; Elementor 1fr=minmax(auto,1fr), auto=min-content, which the global overflow-wrap:anywhere collapses to ~1 char (37px) -> every link wraps 15-21 lines (23-char link=504px tall; footer 17316px vs 942px source). The UNEVEN path (:862-864) ALREADY emits minmax(0,Xfr); the equal path does not. FIX: emit repeat(N,minmax(0,1fr)) for EVERY grid. supabase main-stack is near-1:1 (1.11) — otherwise clean. VERCEL (1.46): GRID-ROW HEIGHT INFLATION (~6357px card-stretch) — buildGrid deletes flex_align_items (line 867) leaving Elementor default align-items:stretch + auto-rows:auto, so every row equalizes to its tallest card (bento 1-tall+6-short=1035px source -> 1929px uniform rows). FIX: grid_align_items:start + grid_auto_rows=median(source card box.h). These are the un-caught 3rd inflation pattern (grid tiles horizontally but inflates vertically). Doing minmax FIRST (safest + biggest single win), align-items SECOND (site-dependent). OUT-OF-SCOPE future lever: vercel headings collapse fs48/40/32 -> 16px (a typography-loss bug).

metricsHistory.target: align-items:start + grid_auto_rows cap for grid card-stretch
metricsHistory.verdict: FLAT + RESTORED — correct+harmless but wrong lever; relocated the real cause

BUT composite-flat -> restored. TWO findings: (1) grid_auto_rows is INERT — Elementor renders grid-auto-rows:auto regardless / kses strips it on the joist PUT (durable). (2) The dominant inflation is NOT row-stretch (which align:start fixes) but OVERFLOWING TALL CHILDREN align:start cannot restrain: vercel grid#5 has a 2592px comparison-table child (vs ~600 captured) -> docH 13470 vs 5918 (2.28x); supabase FOOTER deep-nested link columns stack ~5200px of its 13470. CONVERGED SIGNAL (all 3 agents): on height-SOLVED sites (tailwind hRatio 1.053, linear 1.14) the SOLE remaining lever is COVERAGE — areaCoverage 0.19 starves perElement (tailwind visual stuck 0.567 despite near-perfect height). Region-capture imagery is now THE dominant cross-site lever. build-flow restored to recipe-#17 minmaxkeep state.

metricsHistory.target: categorize the flow areaCoverage gap on tailwind + framer
metricsHistory.verdict: DONE — OVERTURNED the region-capture assumption: both gaps are misclassification/subtree-drop, NOT missing imagery
metricsHistory.note: NEITHER coverage gap needs region-capture — the imagery+text are ALREADY in the capture. TAILWIND (cov 0.607, unmatched 39%): capture-side MISCLASSIFICATION — the hero H1 is captured as kind:code (size16) with CSS class-name labels (text-8xl text-gray-950 tracking-tighter) MERGED into the text -> renders as a tiny garbled <pre> not a 96px heading -> mis-sizes hero + cascades ~750px vertical DRIFT de-aligning ALL downstream regions (that drift, not missing content, is what tanks coverage; 95 text-editors DO render). Fix: capture-layout — do NOT classify sans-serif prose as code (only <pre>/<code>/mono); strip utility-class annotation tokens. FRAMER (cov 0.353, unmatched 65%): build-side SUBTREE DROP — the entire <main> (382 of 434 leaves = 88% of page: 88 img/10 video/266 text) is wrapped in a display:contents div captured as box 0x0; build-flow visibleKid (line 425, w>=3&&h>=2) discards the whole subtree -> clone emits ~50 widgets/0 images. Nodes ARE in capture (stats.coverage 0.96). Fix: build-flow visibleKid — flatten 0x0/display:contents wrappers as transparent pass-throughs (splice children up). CORRECTION to prior model: region-capture imagery is NOT the coverage lever here; misclassification + subtree-drop are.

metricsHistory.target: capture hero as heading not code + strip utility-class tokens
metricsHistory.verdict: REJECTED + RESTORED — regressed BOTH builders; the absolute-3146 no-reg gate correctly caught it
metricsHistory.note: The fix REGRESSED both flow AND absolute -> the in-fix absolute-3146 no-regression gate fired and auto-reverted capture-layout (byte-identical, node --check PASS, selftest 1.0). DEEPER ROOT CAUSE confirmed: tailwind hero is a sans-serif inter text-8xl <h1> (Rapidly build...) wrapped in a container whose innerText is 61.8% VISIBLE plexMono utility-class annotation spans (text-8xl text-gray-950 tracking-tighter text-balance) — those labels are part of the page BY DESIGN (code-as-art hero). So neither stripping them (loses visible content) nor reclassifying the mono-dominated container (regressed both builders) is correct. The right clone is a LAYERED capture (large headline + small positioned annotations as separate sized elements) — complex + tailwind-specific. The absolute no-reg gate is VALIDATED — it prevented a shared-capture change from quietly hurting the production builder.

metricsHistory.target: is the grader clone-capture under-counting (deflating areaCoverage)?
metricsHistory.verdict: DONE — CONFIRMED real + ASYMMETRIC undercount (huge on deep-DOM framer, tiny on shallow tailwind)
metricsHistory.note: FALSE-DEFLATION confirmed. FRAMER: clone renders 488 elements/392 leaves/351 texts but grader resolves only 49 nodes (1 text leaf) — capture-layout MAXD=8 + 40-leaf flatten cap collide with the clone depth-22 Elementor DOM so ONE cap swallows the whole 19403px page. ASYMMETRIC (proven): source captured 96% vs clone 11%; same clone vs a smaller fully-captured source scored coverage 0.61 (10x higher). Impact: areaCoverage 0.048 (vs ~0.65 honest) multiplies perElementScalar ~0.48->0.036, composite ~0.59->0.51. TAILWIND: cap is asymmetric too (fires on flat clone, never nested source — uncapping source = zero change) but deflation is TINY (coverage 0.607->0.622, composite +0.0014) because dropped nodes are small-area text. HONEST CAVEAT: even fully counted framer is NOT 1:1 (responsive 0.32, hRatio 1.568) — the fix corrects metric honesty, not clone quality. FIX = grader-side deep-flatten: when grade-sections captures source+clone, page-spanning wrappers (box.h>0.5*pageH) bypass the 40-cap + harvest all visible descendants (dedup by text@y). ISOLATE to the grader (leave builder-input capture-ensemble capped -> avoids the round-42 builder-clutter regression). Symmetric (source well-nested -> unchanged; clone deep -> counted). Re-baselines the corpus HONESTLY (per grader_strictness_is_progress).

metricsHistory.target: correct the confirmed asymmetric clone-capture false-deflation (grader-side deep-flatten)
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED — real + verified, but PARTIAL magnitude (areaCoverage 0.048->0.121, not the predicted ~0.65)
metricsHistory.note: GRADER-honesty fix ADOPTED (self-test=1.0 BOTH modes, builders UNCHANGED, reversible GRADER_NO_DEEP_FLATTEN=1, independent-VERIFIED). capture-layout deepFlatten opt-in (page-spanning wrappers box.h>0.5*pageH bypass the 40-leaf cap + harvest all visible descendants); grade-sections passes it for BOTH source+clone (symmetric); capture-ensemble (builder input) UNTOUCHED (no round-42 clutter). Framer honest rise: cloneNodes 49->199, matchedPairs 47->189, areaCoverage 0.0482->0.1214, pe-visual 0.036->0.088 — counts REAL widgets (anti-fabrication checked). CRITICAL DOWNSTREAM: prevFlow baselines were on the OLD deflating grader -> RE-BASELINE the flow corpus on the honest grader before more cloner A/Bs (else grader-delta is mis-attributed as cloner gain). metricsHistory.note: SKEPTICAL-BOTH-WAYS result. SUPABASE = CONFIRMED double-penalty: matcher cost has 0.3*centerDist normalized by refH=max(srcH,cloneH), and all sub-scores x areaCoverage. A uniform y-stretch (clone 11262 vs src 7578) inflates refH -> big page-spanning containers fall out of match range -> 27.6M px into unmatchedSrcArea -> areaCoverage 0.087 -> multiplicatively crushes color/text DESPITE raw text=1.0/color 0.56/typo 0.91. ANTI-GAME CONTROL: a SHUFFLED-y clone stays 0.040 (y-norm gives 0.091) -> the fix credits genuine uniform stretch but CANNOT inflate a mis-positioned clone. Magnitude +0.048 on supabase. LINEAR = REFUTED: matchedPairs flat under y-norm (98->97), text-pairs identical (63->63) -> recovers ZERO content; the areaCoverage rise was ONE 14.7M-px background container = phantom. linear low score is an HONEST completeness penalty (clone has 104 nodes vs src 479, 1 text vs 232, 33 imgs vs 8 -> it RASTERIZED ~half the page). FIX (supervised, supabase-class only): two-pass perelement-score — PASS1 match+coverage+color/typo/text on a SINGLE global uniform y-scale (1/hRatio, robustly fit, capped 0.5-2.0); PASS2 position sub-score on RAW boxes so drift is STILL penalized; hRatio+responsive untouched; self-test scale=1 no-op -> source-vs-source stays 1.0. SEPARATE cloner flag: linear flow clone RASTERIZES text (violates words-rebuilt) — a real cloner bug for later.

metricsHistory.target: is absolute-responsive buildable (the biggest router-ceiling lever)?
metricsHistory.verdict: DONE — YES, BUILDABLE + empirically verified; corrected 2 wrong assumptions; path = page custom_css un-pin media query
metricsHistory.note: ABS-RESPONSIVE IS BUILDABLE (verified on throwaway probe page 6377). CORRECTED ASSUMPTIONS: (A) per-breakpoint _tablet/_mobile keys are NOT kses-stripped — they round-trip intact (my earlier overlay-round 'kses strips offset keys' belief was WRONG). (B) flipping children via _position_mobile fails — but NOT kses: _position is not a RESPONSIVE control in Elementor, so _position_mobile is stored yet never compiles; .elementor-absolute welds position:absolute at all widths. REAL PATH: inject ONE scoped media query into page_settings.custom_css (the SAME Pro channel build-absolute already uses for @font-face, proven kses-safe): @media(max-width:1024px){.elementor-absolute{position:relative!important;left/top:auto;width:100%!important}}. VERIFIED on probe: desktop stays absolute pixel-faithful; 768/390 un-pin -> stack vertically, docW==viewport, no h-scroll, custom_css round-tripped (268 chars). FIX (build-absolute.mjs, 3 additive edits): (1) append the un-pin media query to page custom_css; (2) sort emitted leaf widgets by box.y then box.x before root.elements so relative-flow DOM order = visual top-to-bottom; (3) min_height_mobile/_tablet=0 on the root to release the fixed desktop min_height. Desktop (>=1025) UNCHANGED (scoped). EXPECTED: abs responsive 0.04-0.33 -> ~0.40-0.55 (tailwind 0.33->~0.55-0.70), abs corpus 0.625 -> ~0.66-0.69, router ceiling 0.654 -> ~0.68-0.70+. CAVEAT: generic everything->1-col collapse (not source's true 3col->2col->1col regrouping); requires Pro (active). A big step up on responsive, not a 1.0.

metricsHistory.target: Wave-2 #1 (wall B, router primary): port 3->2->1 card-row reflow into build-absolute.
metricsHistory.verdict: REVERTED — but PROBE was definitive + surfaced the dominant mobile bug. supabase composite 0.676->0.637, vercel 0.620->0.605 (both regressed); reflow geometry was CORRECT (3/2/1) but rendered badly (cell min_height retained -> mobile whitespace/bleed) AND the page still overflowed to docW=1440 at mobile due to a SEPARATE pre-existing bug.
metricsHistory.note: PROVEN MECHANISM: grid_columns_grid custom repeat(N,1fr)+_tablet repeat(2)+_mobile repeat(1) COMPILES to real grid-template-columns + reflows clean 3/2/1 on an abs page with desktop pin intact. FLEX _element_custom_width 30/45/100% does NOT work on flex container CHILDREN (their width = --width/_flex_*/content_width; only abs WIDGETS honor _element_custom_width) -> #19528 desktop-custom moot for flex containers. INTEGRATION HAZARDS surfaced: (1) the <=1024 un-pin custom_css selector (.e-con>.elementor-absolute, lines ~595-630) would collapse a grid card-row to 1col below 1024 -> the reflowing row must be EXCLUDED from that selector / be a flow grid container; (2) converting abs cells to grid children changes doc flow (cell min_height + auto rows shift content below) -> breaks desktop-identical on vercel unless the band y/height is preserved + min_height released at mobile; (3) cell-relative leaves keep their desktop left-offset at mobile -> bleed.

metricsHistory.target: Fix absMobileOverflowBug: nav/footer/chrome pinned at 1440px -> mobile horizontal scroll on every abs clone.
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED — 3/3 sites: scrollW@390 1440->390 (overflow ELIMINATED), desktop pixel-identical (vercel 0 diff px, tailwind MD5-identical, supabase 99.997%), responsive term UP (+0.019/+0.045/+0.028), composite UP (+0.004/+0.011/+0.008). No regression.
metricsHistory.note: BROAD mobile-fidelity win on the ROUTER PRIMARY. Root cause: recipe #20 un-pinned the widget WRAPPER (.elementor-absolute->100%) but the INNER html element inside each html-widget kept inline width:<sourceVW>px (=1440) -> doc overflow. Fix un-pins the inner elements at <=1024 + defensive overflow-x:hidden/max-width:100% belt-and-suspenders, scoped @media(max-width:1024px) so desktop is byte-identical. Reversible ABS_NO_CHROMEFIX=1. Avg composite +0.008 / responsive +0.031 across the 3 tested. UNBLOCKS the grid card-row reflow re-test (the no-overflow gate can now pass). NOTE: the manifest means (abs 0.648/flow 0.616) are STALE-GRADER (pre-detectors/block-merge) — current-grader abs sites are ~0.61-0.63; a full corpus re-baseline on the current grader is overdue.

metricsHistory.target: Wall B: grid card-row reflow in build-absolute (container abs-pinned@desktop, reflow@<=1024).
metricsHistory.verdict: REVERTED on composite gate — but BOTH verifiers say it is a REAL responsive WIN false-deflated by the grader. Desktop byte-identical (vercel 99.58%, supabase 99.954% + content-identical 149 leaves same coords), clean 3->2->1, scrollWidth@390=390 no overflow, human-judged better than OFF crude-1col. Yet RLG DIPPED (vercel 0.393->0.370, supabase 0.439->0.429) + supabase per-element pos 0.889->0.716 DESPITE pixel-identical render.
metricsHistory.note: EXPOSES TWO GRADER FALSE-DEFLATION BUGS (the user #5-meta class, deflation direction): (1) RLG responsive metric REWARDS crude everything->1col un-pin OVER true 3->2->1 reflow -> every responsive win (RAM, abs-port, this) scored as flat/down; the objective function prefers WORSE responsive behavior. (2) per-element penalizes grid-NESTING that renders pixel-identically (supabase ON vs OFF render identical + leaf coords identical, yet pos 0.889->0.716, pe 0.8->0.645) -> the matcher is sensitive to DOM nesting depth, not rendered output. The reflow CODE was auto-restored (build-absolute back to chrome-fix-only); reflow approach is reproducible from the workflow. NEXT: diagnose-to-line WHY two pixel-identical clones score differently + WHY RLG prefers 1col, then fix the grader (supervised discipline) so true reflow scores as the win it is -> unblocks the ENTIRE wall-B frontier.

metricsHistory.target: Diagnose+fix graderResponsiveDeflationBug via controlled pixel-identical experiments.
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED (per-element channel) — B2 PROVEN+FIXED in perelement-score.mjs: structurally-invisible layout wrappers (grid container w/ bg==page-default) were counted as unmatched fidelity nodes -> areaCoverage multiplier crushed identical-render clones. Fix: keep a container ONLY if visually distinct (border/radius/shadow OR bg dE>3 vs parent+page). A-vs-B 0.958->1.0, self-test 1.0 both modes, anti-gaming HELD (bad clones DROP 0.472->0.380, stricter not looser), reversible GRADER_STRUCT_INVARIANT=0. B1 (RLG rewards crude-1col) DISPROVEN: RLG(S,true-reflow)=1.0 >= RLG(S,crude-1col)=0.824 — RLG is correctly ordered.
metricsHistory.note: RIGOROUS honest result. B2 fixed in PER-ELEMENT only; the grade-responsive.mjs MIRROR (PROBE_FN area>=2000 textless-box keep) was NOT applied -> the RESPONSIVE 0.25 channel STILL has the structure penalty (diag responsiveAB=0.849 at 1440 where render is identical, due to grid wrapper + cells as extra coverage nodes). The grader objective is HALF-fixed. NEXT: mirror the distinctness gate into grade-responsive PROBE_FN (same flag) to complete it, THEN re-run the card-row reflow (now both channels structure-invariant) to confirm it scores as a win. B1 disproven means real-clone reflow drops at narrow widths are REAL differences (worth fixing in the BUILDER), not grader bugs.

metricsHistory.target: Mirror the per-element struct-invariant gate into grade-responsive PROBE_FN to complete the objective fix.
metricsHistory.verdict: REVERTED — gate works on-target (A-vs-B responsive 0.849->1.0, RLG(S,C)=1.0>=RLG(S,D)=0.822, anti-inflation STRONG 0.216->0.098) BUT breaks the HARD self-test rail NONDETERMINISTICALLY: 4 self-test runs = 0.9525/0.9269/0.9921/0.9929, 2/4 FAIL the >=0.96 threshold. Adversarial verify ran self-test 4x + caught it.
metricsHistory.note: Root cause: hasDistinctSignal (offsetParent bg compare + lazy/IntersectionObserver content materialization) makes two INDEPENDENT captures of the same URL keep DIFFERENT borderline wrapper subsets -> coverage 0.945-0.995 -> responsiveScore dips below the 0.96 rail. The gate amplifies grade-responsives inherent re-capture nondeterminism. A flaky grader is worse than a stable slightly-deflated one -> correct revert. FOLLOW-UP (queued, not abandoned): a DETERMINISTIC distinctness decision (strict prune of ONLY obviously-invisible wrappers bg==parent exactly + no border/radius/shadow, so borderline cases that flip between captures are consistently KEPT). For now the per-element struct-invariant fix (the BIGGER channel) stands; the responsive 0.25 channel keeps its smaller structure penalty.

metricsHistory.target: Wave-3 #2 (wall B responsive-type): clamp() fluid fonts in build-absolute for large text.
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED (recipe #22) — 3/3 sites: desktop byte-identical (vercel 100% 0 diff px, tailwind 99.9996%, supabase 99.74%), mobile headings scale (40/36->25/22, 48/40/32->30/25/20), docH@390 DOWN (less inflation), composite no-reg (supabase +0.001). Reversible ABS_NO_FLUIDFONT=1.
metricsHistory.note: 4TH keep of the session. Real human-visible mobile win (giant desktop headings -> readable mobile, hero wraps 2->1 line) at ZERO desktop cost (clamp(MIN, MAX/1440*100vw, MAX) == MAX at 1440). BUT the grader composite is FLAT — the verifier confirmed: the RLG responsive 0.25 dimension measures edge-set/layout GEOMETRY, not font-size or overflow or height, so it does NOT reward fluid fonts. SESSION PATTERN: chrome-unpin, reflow, fluid-fonts are all real mobile improvements the grader under-measures because the responsive dimension ignores the human-obvious mobile defects (horizontal OVERFLOW, oversized TEXT, HEIGHT inflation). NEXT: add a deterministic, source-relative MOBILE-PROPORTION fidelity term to grade-responsive (overflow + height-ratio per breakpoint) so the grader honestly measures mobile fidelity + the real work registers.

metricsHistory.target: Add deterministic source-relative mobile-proportion (overflow + height-ratio) to grade-responsive.
metricsHistory.verdict: REVERTED (v1) — DETERMINISTIC (selftest5 all PASS, mobileProp exactly 1.0 x5, NOT flaky) + PENALIZES real defects (overflow clone 0.277 < clean 0.437; grader now SEES overflow+inflation) BUT content-blind -> GAMEABLE: a wrong-content clone got +0.134 (>0.02 ceiling) from matching proportions alone.
metricsHistory.note: Adversarial verify caught a structural gameability hole: the mobile-prop term reads only scrollWidth/docH (never node identity), so a wrong-content clone that happens not to overflow + has comparable inflated docH earns undeserved proportion credit (mobileProp 0.605 * 0.3 blend lifts RLG 0.1725 -> 0.3024). FIX (verify-specified): coverage-WEIGHT the term (multiply mobileProportion by the same coverage F1 the RLG already applies to edge/layout) so low-coverage wrong-content clones cannot earn it. Self-test (coverage=1) + overflow-penalty (overflow=0 regardless) untouched. Re-running v2 with the coverage-weight. The overflow + height halves are both HONEST + DETERMINISTIC — only the content-gating was missing.

metricsHistory.target: v2 coverage-weighted mobile-proportion term.
metricsHistory.verdict: REVERTED (self-RESTORED) — blocked by grade-responsive INHERENT self-test flakiness near the >=0.96 rail (independent re-capture variance), NOT the terms fault (v1 proved mobileProp deterministic == 1.0 x5).
metricsHistory.note: ROOT CAUSE (pre-existing, latent): grade-responsive --selftest re-captures the SAME url TWICE independently -> RLG node-matching varies 0.957-0.999 -> occasionally breaches the EPS=0.04 (>=0.96) rail. The deterministic mobile-prop term got caught by this. This blocks ANY responsive-grader change that gets self-tested. PREREQUISITE for landing mobile-prop: make grade-responsive self-test deterministic (compare a capture to ITSELF, not two independent re-captures) OR widen EPS to reflect real re-capture variance (~0.92). DECISION: STOP the grader/responsive cluster (4 keeps + diminishing returns + flakiness walls); re-baseline the corpus on the current honest grader to re-ground + objectively target the biggest remaining gap.

metricsHistory.target: Re-ground: true corpus mean on the current honest grader + ranked top defect.
metricsHistory.verdict: COMPLETE 7/7 — TRUE corpus mean (current honest grader, build-absolute): composite 0.5801, visual 0.6254, editability 0.8554, structural 0.4973, responsive 0.3625 (FLOOR). #1 lever (objectively): responsive VERTICAL-reflow (7/8 sites); #2: dynamic-content capture (reactdev/resend/framer).
metricsHistory.note: Per-site: tailwind 0.639, supabase 0.592, resend 0.553, framer 0.529, reactdev 0.533 (+linear/vercel). UNAMBIGUOUS #1: responsive vertical-reflow — chrome-fix solved HORIZONTAL overflow (scrollWidth390=390 ALL sites) but VERTICALLY abs widgets retain desktop pixel top/height when un-pinned -> mobile docH balloons 2.1x(tailwind)-9.5x(framer) vs source -> near-empty 390 column. responsive 0.3625 is the floor + worst on 7/8. recipe #20 un-pin clearly does position:relative+width:100% but does NOT reset the desktop top-offset/height -> cumulative gaps. ranked recommendation: spend next 2-3 rounds on responsive-reflow. #2 structural-capture (dynamic React/animated sections lost on reactdev 0.34/resend 0.337/framer 0.383). editability strong (0.855, dont chase).

metricsHistory.target: THE #1 corpus lever (responsive 0.3625 floor, 7/8 sites): fix vertical mobile reflow in build-absolute recipe #20.
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED (recipe #23) — 3/3 sites compact + desktop byte-identical. Mobile docH ratio crushed: tailwind 2.21x->0.95x (NAILED ~source), supabase 7.57x->3.11x (-59%), framer 9.51x->4.76x (-50%). properMobileStack=true all 3. responsive UP tailwind 0.349->0.387 + framer 0.342->0.394; composite UP tailwind 0.638->0.648 + framer 0.529->0.541; supabase flat (within band). Reversible ABS_NO_VREFLOW=1.
metricsHistory.note: 5TH keep + highest-leverage. DIAGNOSIS: retainedFixedHeight (NOT top-offset; cssTop=0) — recipe #20 un-pinned the wrapper but never reset height/min-height on the INNER elements; baked inline height:Npx on bgRect divs + role=main/banner/footer landmark twins kept desktop band heights -> 2-9.5x balloon. FIX: height:auto/min-height:0 on the wrapper + .elementor-widget-container>* + role-landmark twins + transform:none, scoped @media(max-width:1024px). tailwind NAILED (0.95x); supabase/framer HALVED (residual 3.1/4.8x = decorative bg-rect CONTAINERS the widget-scoped rule does not catch -> 2nd reflow round lever). RLG under-credits docH compaction (supabase composite flat) -> the mobile-prop term (blocked by grade-responsive self-test flakiness) is needed to fully register this. BACKEND QUIRK banked: custom_css-only changes (unchanged element tree) return 422 atomic_save_silent_failure (Joist Check A reads unchanged-tree as silent-drop on Elementor 4.0.9); workaround = empty-then-rebuild the tree.

metricsHistory.target: KEYSTONE: fix grade-responsive self-test determinism (deepcopy) + land coverage-weighted mobile-prop term.
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED (recipe #24) — self-test EXACTLY 1.0 x5 (deepcopy root-fix, spread 0); mobile-prop credits real mobile (vreflow tailwind 0.1436->0.1698) + PENALIZES defects AND FLIPS a wrong ranking (overflow clone raw-RLG 0.355 > clean 0.296, but mobile-prop ranks overflow 0.261 < clean 0.294 — proves it adds signal the RLG was blind to); anti-inflation held (wrong-content +0.009 < 0.02, coverage-gated); reversible GRADER_NO_MOBILEPROP=1.
metricsHistory.note: 6TH keep + KEYSTONE. Two wins: (A) grade-responsive self-test now TRULY deterministic (SELFTEST clone = deepcopy of source -> EXACTLY 1.0; the independent-recapture was conflating grader-consistency with live-site variance -> flaky 0.957-0.999 that blocked mobile-prop twice). This unblocks ALL future responsive-grader changes. (B) responsive dimension now HONESTLY measures mobile (overflow + height-ratio, coverage-weighted blend 0.7*RLG+0.3*mobileProp*cov). The session mobile wins (chrome-unpin/fluid-fonts/vreflow) now REGISTER + future reflow is gradeable. RESOLVES graderResponsiveDeflationBug + graderResponsiveSelftestFlaky.

metricsHistory.target: Wave-4 #1 click-to-reveal capture for the structural floor (dynamic-content).
metricsHistory.verdict: NOT BUILT — diagnose-first proved click-to-reveal is NOT a real lever (reactdev 0 genuine click-gated leaves; resend 22 = 5.5%, mostly nav mega-menu chrome the clone does not need). Saved a wasted build round.
metricsHistory.note: Diagnose-first GATE worked perfectly + caught its own false positives: the earlier '67 click-gated' on reactdev was a nav-link NAVIGATING to /blog (counted the whole Blog page); resend's raw 67 was SVG position-shift artifacts on tab-reflow. De-noised: reactdev 0 click-gated (all 366 leaves render on load; the 8 Sandpack editors show code+preview already-visible), resend 22 mostly nav-dropdown chrome. THE STRUCTURAL GAP IS ELSEWHERE: (1) canvas / Sandpack-IFRAME / live-React-output PREVIEW surfaces render visually but expose NO walkable DOM text -> capture drops them -> sparse sections; (2) decorative-SVG vs content misclassification (147 'media leaves' largely decorative SVG). LEVER: region-raster these cohesive visual SURFACES as element-level images (canvas/iframe/sparse-painted), with GPU/ANGLE flags + a blank-raster guard, + better SVG-vs-content classification. Aligns with user principle (element-level images OK, words rebuilt; never chunk a ROW of distinct things).

metricsHistory.target: Structural floor: region-raster cohesive visual surfaces (canvas/iframe) as element-level images.
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED (recipe #25) WITH CAVEAT — resend visual 0.539->0.644 (+0.105, void->content), composite 0.496->0.52; canvas renders headless (no GPU flag), React previews are cross-origin iframes (rastered); blank-guard held (no black); no-op reactdev/tailwind. BUT struct DROPPED 0.444->0.302 (-0.142) + the raster list includes the hero heading 'Email for developers' -> WORD-BURYING RISK (violates user principle words-rebuilt).
metricsHistory.note: Real visual win (resend hero/cards: black voids -> real content) on canvas/cross-origin-iframe surfaces, correctly inert where content is walkable (reactdev Sandpack=same-origin CodeMirror DOM -> 0 rastered). Diagnose: canvasRendersHeadless=true, gpuFlagHelps=false, previews cross-origin iframes. CONCERN: struct -0.142 + a heading in the raster set strongly implies it rasters surfaces that CONTAIN text (burying words in the image) instead of only text-free visual units. The composite-up gate did NOT check word-preservation. NEXT ROUND (priority): audit whether surface-raster buries any native text on resend; TIGHTEN so it only rasters TEXT-FREE visual surfaces (pure canvas/iframe) and for surfaces with overlaid text, keep the text as native leaves (mask+rebuild) — never bury words. Reversible CAPTURE_NO_SURFACERASTER=1. Also: honester grader (mobile-prop #24) lowered resend re-baseline 0.553->~0.496 (correctly penalizing resend bad mobile) — surface-raster recovers some.

metricsHistory.target: Audit+tighten surface-raster (recipe #25) for word-burying (user principle: words rebuilt).
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED (recipe #26) — concern CONFIRMED + FIXED. Audit proved the hero H1 'Email for developers' was baked into an image (gradient-text H1, descendant-only text-gate scored it 0, 3 words < 8 threshold). Tightened the gate to count the element's OWN text + bail on any heading>=2 words + text-area ratio -> skip-and-recurse so text emits native. VERIFIED: H1 now a native selectable heading (not in any image); all 20 headings native; 10 text-free visual surfaces STILL rastered (recovery retained); composite 0.52->0.517 (flat). Honors words-rebuilt WHILE keeping canvas/iframe visual recovery.
metricsHistory.note: 8TH keep + a CORRECTNESS win the composite cannot see: traded a rastered headline-glyph-region for a real EDITABLE <h1> at net-neutral score (0.517 vs 0.52). The 'judge like a human, not the number' principle made concrete — the clone is genuinely better (editable heading, no buried words) even though composite is flat. struct stays 0.302 (the honest unwalkable-surface cost for the 10 genuine canvas/iframe surfaces remains — correct). Reversible CAPTURE_NO_SURFACERASTER=1. surfaceRasterWordBuryingConcern RESOLVED.

metricsHistory.target: Test the uncapped responsive lever: re-baseline build-flow + best-per-site router.
metricsHistory.verdict: NOT ADOPTED (router marginal) — flow corpus mean 0.5231 (< abs); flow RESPONSIVE 0.3129 (> abs 0.2296, +0.083, as predicted) BUT flow VISUAL+STRUCTURAL lower (container-inference less pixel-faithful than abs pinning) -> net flow composite ~= or < abs. Best-per-site ROUTER wins flow on only 2/7 (supabase 0.564>0.553, linear 0.487>0.46) for ~+0.005. Routing to flow does NOT cheaply lift the corpus.
metricsHistory.note: HONEST negative that kills the route-to-flow hypothesis: flow's reflow advantage (responsive +0.083) is OFFSET by its visual/structural loss vs abs's pixel-pinning. Per-site flow comp: tailwind 0.573, supabase 0.564, resend 0.515, framer 0.509, linear 0.487, vercel 0.499, reactdev 0.515. The responsive floor (0.2296) is NOT cheaply fixable by routing — it needs a HYBRID (abs fidelity + flow reflow) or the structure-inference rework (wave-4 #2, heavier track), or accept it as capped. CONSEQUENCE: the re-baseline OVERWROTE all 7 pages with flow builds; they get rebuilt (abs) as I work each site. PIVOT to the ADDRESSABLE levers: text-collision (user #4, cheap, flagship) + dynamic-content (broad).

metricsHistory.target: USER #4: remove duplicated/overlapping text on supabase (doubled nav/CTA labels).
metricsHistory.verdict: FIX WORKS but AUTO-REVERTED by a MIS-DESIGNED GATE. Diagnosis nailed the root (capture-layout emits each label TWICE: wrapper /
metricsHistory.note: GATE-DESIGN LESSON (banked): do NOT gate on a metric the grader does not measure for that defect class. The user's #4 doubled-text is HUMAN-VISIBLE but UNGRADED (grader skips same-text overlap by design, to not penalize legit wrapper/inner). So this fix's value is human-visible + editability (one button not two) at FLAT composite — another judge-like-a-human case. The grader's collisionRate (0.0946) measures DIFFERENT-text containment (testimonial body box swallowing an author handle / like-count), NOT the doubled labels. RE-APPLYING with a corrected gate (dupLabelsGone + composite no-reg + self-test 1.0 + no-over-dedupe; DROP the collisionRate requirement). Root NOT in build-absolute (it 1:1-pins capture); fix is capture-layout leaf() overlap-aware same-text dedupe.

metricsHistory.target: USER #4: remove duplicated/overlapping/garbled text on supabase.
metricsHistory.verdict: ADOPTED (recipe #27) — USER #4 CLOSED. supabase collisionRate 0.0946->0, composite 0.551->0.563 (+0.012), visual 0.574->0.6 (+0.026); dup labels gone (screenshots); tailwind no-reg 0.591->0.595; self-test 1.0. Reversible ABS_NO_DEDUPE=1 / ABS_NO_OVERLAPFIX=1.
metricsHistory.note: 9TH keep + closes the LAST of the user's original visible feedback. v2 re-diagnosis found THREE distinct defects + fixed all in build-absolute.mjs (lines 926-1013): (1) SAME-TEXT doubled labels (7 wrapper+inner twins dropped -> one Start-your-project/Request-a-demo/nav each — the human-visible doubling); (2) DIFFERENT-TEXT CONTAINMENT collisions (full-width intro para box swallows testimonial @handle; quote box swallows overlaid 103.6K stat — real z-layered DOM the abs builder flattens to siblings keeping full boxes) -> a box-CLIP pre-pass trims the over-tall containing box to its own single-line height (breaks containment, drops NO leaf, all text editable at captured x,y) -> collisionRate->0; (3) hero heading WRAP-overlap (nowrap guard on single-line leaves). The grader counts different-text containment (so this registered: composite +0.012) but skips same-text (so the doubled-labels fix is the human-visible-only part). metricsHistory.note: HARD EVIDENCE of a grader false-deflation: ALL reactdev per-element sub-scores are EXCELLENT raw (color 0.8276, typo 0.9474, pos 0.9002, text 0.9902, effects 0.6557) but ALL multiplied down by areaCoverage 0.1931 -> reported ~0.17. The grader CONFLATES accuracy (raw sub-scores, high) with completeness (coverage, low: 82/205 matched). reactdev is ACCURATE but INCOMPLETE, and the multiply hides that. Bankables: inline-color spans DO survive kses (byte-for-byte round-trip); code emitted as html widget (bypasses text-editor kses) -> code-token-color feasible LATER but NOT the bottleneck (raw color already 0.83). THE LEVER is grader #6 re-attribution: report coverage SEPARATELY + score sub-scores on RAW per-pair means -> un-crush accuracy + name completeness as its own dimension. Then the real gap (capture missing content / matcher coverage) is visible + targetable.

metricsHistory.target: #6 keystone: un-crush accuracy + coverage as a separate penalized dimension.
metricsHistory.verdict: REVERTED — GAMEABLE (anti-gaming rail failed). Adversarial verify proved: self-tests 1.0 (all modes), accuracy un-crushed (reactdev per-element 0.169->0.864 toward raw 0.85), reversible, no NaN — BUT un-gating accuracy inflates accurate-incomplete clones (reactdev +0.054, adversarial +0.031, 1-node-perfect worst-case +0.17 lifting 0.32->0.49). Precise model: Delta=(1-c)*(0.175a - 0.20S) — the coverage penalty moved from 0.175-in-visual to only 0.20-on-structural, weakening it.
metricsHistory.note: DECISIVE CONCLUSION: the composite (accuracy x completeness, via the coverage-multiply) IS the HONEST overall-fidelity number — an incomplete clone IS genuinely lower-fidelity. Un-gating accuracy is gameable; fully cancelling the un-gate leaves the composite unchanged -> no point changing it. The #6 VALUE was the ATTRIBUTION insight (now banked): per-element ACCURACY is excellent (~0.85 across the corpus); the gap is COMPLETENESS (82/205 matched). So STOP tuning the grader here + ATTACK COMPLETENESS directly — which, under the existing coverage-multiply, AMPLIFIES into a large composite gain (coverage 0.19->0.4 ~doubles the per-element contribution). pe.raw{} already holds the raw sub-scores for steering. Next = completeness lever (capture missing content OR reduce over-counted decorative fragments), diagnose-first.

Also: build-time color-snap (matched-pair text color -> source color; 'scale to millions' src green rgb(63,207,142)) + font-size-snap (typo 0.4633->0.4659 when nudged); inline-style-rewrite on the inner div is the correct mechanism (wins over widget settings + theme CSS). metricsHistory.target: Lift the per-element typo cap (~0.46) via build-time font-family assignment.

The honest lever (per #6: do NOT un-gate, gameable) = RECOVER MORE REAL SOURCE CONTENT so more area MATCHES (dynamic-content-emit proved this: +0.0225 by recovering text bands). grader family compare = case/quote-normalized first-token equality, family weight 0.50. metricsHistory.note: 4TH + DEEPEST coverage finding: the composite (~0.56) SIGNIFICANTLY UNDERSTATES true fidelity. The cloner reproduces the content (images uploaded+rendered at full box, dashboards, illustrations, card grids) but the grader's strict 1:1 Hungarian matcher STARVES co-located STACKED nodes — each region has multiple stacked nodes on both sides (wrapper+inner+svg+image+text), only min(N,M) pair, the rest dump into unmatched even though pixels are identical. Plus source flatten keeps bg-containers with no clone-side container counterpart (clone has an image/video widget there, not kind:container) -> can never pair. This is a FALSE-DEFLATION (user #1 priority). HONEST FIX (diag-specified, SAFE): a SYMMETRIC container-COALESCE pre-pass — collapse a stack of same-box wrapper containers into their innermost content leaf on BOTH sides before the cost matrix (struct-invariance extended; analogous to blockMerge). Self-test 1.0 by construction (source-vs-source identical). ANTI-GAMING: an incomplete clone must NOT gain coverage (its missing content's source nodes still have no clone counterpart). Minor side: unmatchedCloneJunk small (1.7%) — leaked 8x8 wave-fallback bgRects; builder cleanup later, not the throttle. NEXT = supervised matcher-coalesce round (the honest composite unlock).

metricsHistory.target: Symmetric container-coalesce to stop 1:1-matcher starvation of reproduced stacked content.
metricsHistory.verdict: REVERTED (v1) on anti-gaming — but NEAR-MISS with a precise fix. Coalesce is SOUND + symmetric: self-test 1.0 all 3 modes, reversible, no NaN, render-identical-nesting EQUAL (deep/flat 1.0 ON vs 0.88 OFF). GENUINE coverage rise supabase 0.491->0.610 composite 0.59->0.606 (folds 2 redundant textless wrappers -> video pairs 1:1, content verified rendered at box). BUT incomplete-clone rose +0.0246 > 0.02 gate.
metricsHistory.note: Verify was HONEST (FLAGged vs rationalized). The gaming is NOT crediting missing content (matchedPairs DECREASED 98->97, zero new matches) — it is a bounded SOURCE-side DENOMINATOR artifact: a missing region triple-counted as unmatched (wrapper+inner+video ~2.1M px2) gets de-double-counted by coalesce (~680K) -> denominator shrinks -> coverage rises slightly w/o a new match. Complete-clone gain (+0.12 real matches) DWARFS incomplete (+0.0246 denom-only); discrimination STRENGTHENS. V2 FIX (verify-specified): folded wrapper AREA contributes 0 to the coverage calc (neither matched-eligible nor unmatched-denominator) — only surviving CONTENT leaves count -> a missing region keeps full denominator weight (no discount) -> incomplete-clone gate <=0.02. Did NOT loosen the 0.02 gate (honesty > rationalizing). Re-running v2.

metricsHistory.target: v2: coalesce + folded-wrappers-contribute-0-area to fix the v1 anti-gaming artifact.
metricsHistory.verdict: REVERTED (self-RESTORED, clean) — even WITH the denominator fix (folded wrappers removed from all 3 area terms), it passed self-test 1.0 both modes but STILL FAILED anti-gaming. Two careful attempts both fail the same gate -> coalesce is fundamentally NOT anti-gaming-clean. Base verified clean (coalesce=0, self-test 1.0); restored from /tmp/ev-TRUSTED-perelem-clean.mjs.
metricsHistory.note: CONCLUSION of the coverage/grader thread (6 rounds: #6, completeness, font-family, coverage-recovery, coalesce v1+v2): per-element ACCURACY is excellent (raw ~0.94); the areaCoverage throttle (~0.49) is ~100% matcher-STARVATION of reproduced stacked content (content IS rendered at the same box). So the composite MODESTLY UNDERSTATES true fidelity. BUT crediting it via a matcher/coverage change is fundamentally gameable (can't distinguish stacked-reproduced from stacked-partially-missing without per-region match verification = the gaming surface; 2 disciplined attempts both fail anti-gaming). DECISION: ACCEPT the composite as an HONEST CONSERVATIVE UNDER-COUNT (the SAFE direction; user-validated 'stricter=good'); leave the grader at trusted-clean; STOP grader rounds. The cloner is genuinely BETTER than ~0.56 shows. PIVOT to unambiguous scalable levers (roadmap): synthetic bench (kills the variance that wasted most of this session), then responsive (the biggest real dimension gap).

metricsHistory.note: DEFINITIVE coverage root cause + the LIVE-SAFE fix (the clean salvage of #29). ROOT: build-absolute emits a full-bleed GRADIENT background as a childless div with only an 8px probe -> capture isCssBgSurface fires (realMedia < 24px floor) -> grader re-captures it as a phantom kind:mockup 1440x501 surface -> source band is a CONTAINER -> no pair -> 721K unmatched-clone area -> areaCoverage 0.3225 crushes every sub-score. FIX (diag-VERIFIED LIVE): make the full-bleed gradient/image bgRect probe child >=24px (opacity 0.06, pointer-events:none) -> capture sees realMedia>=1 -> isCssBgSurface=FALSE -> band recurses as a CONTAINER matching the source -> areaCoverage 0.3225->0.9976, hero visual 0.607->0.886, SSIM UNCHANGED. This is #29s PROBE arm ALONE (additive, never removes a panel) — NOT the suppression arm (that blanked tailwind content, net-negative). SCOPE the probe to FULL-BLEED section bg-bands (gradient/section), NOT content rasters, so it never flips a real source mockup. NEXT = ship the 24px-probe arm scoped+alone, default-ON, double-gated (bench+live). Residual smaller follow-up: system-font-stack typo (raw 0.497, source system-ui falls to Inter).

metricsHistory.verdict: MAJOR DISCOVERY (not a clone defect): the vercel SSIM-0.019 catastrophe is a CAPTURE/GRADER THEME-EMULATION MISMATCH. vercel ships <meta color-scheme="dark light"> (dark-preferred, theme-adaptive). capture-layout.mjs was FORCE-emulating colorScheme:dark -> captured vercels dark design -> clone rendered dark (root bg rgb 8,8,8). But the grader renders its SOURCE REFERENCE under headless default = LIGHT (rgb 250,250,250). Dark clone vs light reference -> ~95% inverted pixels -> SSIM 0.019. The clone was FAITHFUL; the two sides disagreed on theme.

Only affects theme-ADAPTIVE sites (dark-only sites render dark under both arms, untouched).

metricsHistory.rootCause: Build-side STALE POSITIONAL-FILENAME ASSET CACHE. capture-layout writes each source <svg> to a DOM-order positional temp file (/tmp/svg-<srcTag>-<N>.png) -- capture is FAITHFUL. But build-absolute upload cache imgMap was keyed by that positional FILENAME, so once a prior structurally-different capture uploaded e.g. svg-linearapp-0.png (a Vercel mark, WP id 4324), every later build got a cache HIT -> uploadImage early-returned -> the WP asset stayed FROZEN at the wrong content (svg-resendcom-4=php chip, -5=Go chip). Explains wrong logos corpus-wide.
metricsHistory.fix: build-absolute.mjs (+23 lines): content-address the cache key for LOCAL temp rasters -> <path>#<sha1-of-content>, so identical-path-but-different-content files no longer collide. Reversible.

metricsHistory.rootCause: The existing text-collision de-dupe gated on EXACT text equality, so it missed the real defect: a parent text leaf whose innerText is the CONCATENATION of its inline child leaves (capture flattens inline spans -> parent + children are sibling leaves at containment-1.0 overlapping boxes). Parent text superset of child but not exact -> 0 of 64 linear + 1 resend genuine duplicates dropped -> every glyph painted twice.
metricsHistory.fix: build-absolute.mjs +49 lines: additive 2nd de-dupe pass (after exact-match) detecting concat-twins (parent P + contained sibling child C, containment>0.8, C-text strict substring of P) -> drop redundant concat-PARENT (children carry glyphs) or re-painted child spans (parent carries full text); preserves ALL text. Reversible ABS_NO_CONCAT_DEDUPE=1.

The tiler cut BOTH at the same fixed pixel-row -> paired non-corresponding content (resend 26/27: source cell black-void vs clone full feature grid) -> FALSE "different region" penalty understating drift-y sites.

Reversible --anchor off. + permanent regression test _vtiles-selftest.mjs exercising the exact CLI functions.

T2 drift: anchored source~=clone (pdiff 0.0 vs fixed 84.7) -> drift-present content paired. metricsHistory.what: REVERTED the content-anchored tiling (de-deflation #4 was WRONGLY recorded KEPT off the self-test). REAL-PAGE A/B is decisive: anchored REGRESSES both sites -- linear 64.0->47.5 (-16.5), resend 61.7->49.9 (-11.8), pervasive false content-misalignment across 16-24 tiles. Global landmark-anchoring mis-pairs ambiguous/repeated text on real pages -> source crops pulled to wrong y -> breaks the aligned MAJORITY far worse than the few real drift bands it fixed.
metricsHistory.lesson: SELF-TEST INSUFFICIENT: T1/T2/T3 covered controlled cases (identity, ONE drift band, ONE void) but NOT the real-page failure mode (ambiguous landmark matching across a page of similar text). A grader change MUST be A/B-validated on REAL pages BEFORE recording KEPT. I jumped to KEPT on the self-test; the real-page re-grade then exposed the regression. Reverted default to fixed-y; anchoring kept behind --anchor on for future refinement.
metricsHistory.trueNumbers: HONEST numbers stand at the validated FIXED-Y tiler: linear 64.0 (+14 from 50), resend 61.7 (+13.1 from 48.6). resend drift tiles 25/26/27 = a real-but-MINOR false-penalty (~3 tiles), acceptable; NOT worth the fragile global-anchoring fix.

metricsHistory.rootCause: codePanelWidget (build-absolute.mjs:523) UNCONDITIONALLY synthesized a solid dark #0b0d10 panel surface whenever n.bg==null. capture-layout mono-dominance branch (1092-1103) over-classifies tailwindcss.com's tiny INLINE monospace utility-class annotation labels (text-8xl / text-gray-950 / text-balance ...) as kind:'code'; those carry NO captured panel bg, so the render stamped a BLACK BOX behind faint near-invisible gray text. Card-chrome and band-bg do NOT fire on these leaves (confirmed: annotations carry no border/shadow/bg signal). Box HEIGHT is the reliable discriminator: annotations h=40-96, genuine panels h=412-578.
metricsHistory.fix: Render-side ANNOTATION-LABEL GUARD in build-absolute.mjs ONLY (reversible BUILD_NO_CODE_ANNOT_GUARD): synthesize the dark surface only for a GENUINE code panel -- genuine = hasCapturedBg || box.h>=150 || lineCount>=6. Non-genuine annotation leaves render as faint NATIVE monospace text (transparent, no box) using the captured text color. Refines fix #3 (codePanel) / #5 (card-chrome).

metricsHistory.lever: FRESH CLEAN LEVER (t12): react.dev white-pill SECONDARY CTA ('</> Add React to your page') lost its white-pill chrome + leading </> code-icon -> clone renders plain BOLD TEXT. Root: fix #10 buttonPaint rejects white-on-light buttons (anti-false-pill guard accepts only solid-fill/gradient/visible-border, NOT shadow-elevated white pills). Recoverable: extend buttonPaint to accept a button with a genuine captured box-shadow (elevation) even when bg is white/near-page, with strict anti-false-pill guards (shadow||border AND padding AND short-text link/button), so white pills render WITHOUT sprouting false pills on plain links/nav.
metricsHistory.secondary: Lower-priority/deferred: nav logo-icon replaced by text + nav search-pill missing (interactive/functional, partly inherent).

metricsHistory.rootCause: TWO-part. (1) CAPTURE BUG (the real find): capture-layout nz() used a ^-anchored regex that matched the LEADING transparent layer of a multi-layer Tailwind 'ring' box-shadow (rgba(0,0,0,0) 0 0..., rgba(0,0,0,0) 0 0..., rgb(217,219,227) 0 0 0 1px inset) and zeroed the ENTIRE shadow -> boxShadow:null at capture. This silently killed ALL multi-layer box-shadows corpus-wide, not just this button. (2) BUILD: with bg/border/shadow all null + tag=a, the leaf hit none of buttonPaint's fill/border/tag signals -> returned null -> bare-anchor plain-text fallback.
metricsHistory.fix: capture-layout.mjs: added shadowOf() -- splits computed box-shadow into layers (commas OUTSIDE parens), drops fully-transparent + zero-geometry layers, keeps meaningful layer(s); replaced nz(cs.boxShadow) with shadowOf(...) on button/heading leaves. build-absolute.mjs: extended buttonPaint to accept a shadow-elevated white/near-page pill (emit white bg + captured shadow + radius + padding). Reversible BUILD_NO_WHITEPILL.
metricsHistory.antiGaming: CRITICAL distinguishing signal: reactdev nav items (Learn/Reference/Community/Blog) ALSO have radius:9999px + padding 6/12 + short text -- a naive 'padding+radius+short-text' guard would invent 4+ spurious nav pills. The TRUE separator is the INSET RING box-shadow (rgb(217,219,227) 0 0 0 1px inset) which nav links DO NOT have. Guard requires that genuine captured shadow||border, so nav links stay plain.
metricsHistory.gate: reactdev 'Add React to your page' renders full white-pill chrome: bg rgb(255,255,255), boxShadow rgb(217,219,227) 0 0 0 1px inset (exact source ring), radius 9999px, pad 10px 24px (screenshot-verified). NO false pills on plain links/nav across reactdev+resend+linear+supabase; existing #10 filled CTAs intact. No h-scroll; scoped (only build-absolute.mjs + capture-layout.mjs; grade/perelement byte-identical); node --check; reversible. Adversarial reviewer VERIFIED both directions.
metricsHistory.impact: CORPUS-WIDE capture improvement: shadowOf() restores ALL multi-layer box-shadows (Tailwind ring composites etc.) that nz() was silently zeroing -> better elevation/chrome fidelity across many sites, not just reactdev's secondary CTA. Biggest-reach fix of the recent arc.
metricsHistory.caveat: </> leading icon DEFERRED (separate SVG/icon, not cheaply captured) -- did not block. Working tree also carries #11's uncommitted codePanelWidget guard (pre-existing, already KEPT, unrelated) -- commit only when user asks.

metricsHistory.overlapMode: (b) BUILDER-COLLAPSE, not a capture error. The overlap is in the FOOTER 'Learn React' docs-topics link-grid (~y8800-9070), NOT a section header. Capture was FAITHFUL (each link leaf h31, lh30px, distinct y, no overlap, matching source docH 9097). The builder emitted footer-link <a> leaves (kind:button) pinned to single-line glyph width with ZERO slack and NO white-space:nowrap -> multi-word labels ('Managing State' h31->h47 wrap, 'Escape Hatches') wrapped to 2 lines and OVERFLOWED onto the next link (Managing State y8978+60=9038 overlapped Escape Hatches y9010, yov28/xov99).
metricsHistory.rootCause: Footer link columns are detected as a card-row and emitted by emitCardRow->leafWidget BEFORE the dedupe-block guards run, so a guard placed there marked the tree node TOO LATE to reach the already-emitted grid-cell widget. Also the existing headline guard's fontSize-based single-line test mis-classed line-height-driven link boxes.
metricsHistory.fix: NEW markLinkWrapGuard() run right after detectHeaderNav and BEFORE detectCardRows; the button branch honors _noWrap (white-space:nowrap). Guards: single-line iff box.h ~= line-height (0.5*lh <= box.h <= 1.5*lh, replacing the fontSize-based test), multi-WORD only (single-word cannot wrap), link/button leaves. Reversible flag. Files: build-absolute.mjs (+113) + capture-layout.mjs (+24).

metricsHistory.discovery: GRADER-HONESTY BUG: the vision-tiler (grade-vision-tiles.mjs) screenshots the CLONE without scroll-triggering the clone's OWN lazy-loaded webp images (photos at y7728 load on scroll/view) -> tile-26 captured WHITE SPACE where real photos exist = a FALSE content-void. This is a tiler-side false-void (the clone is fine; the SCOREBOARD lied). Connects to [void_detector_dark_band_falsepositive] + [clone_validation_pitfalls] (screenshot-LOOK caught what the tile claimed). The tiler likely scrolls the SOURCE but not the CLONE far enough, or full-page-screenshots before lazy webp paint.
metricsHistory.infra: The workflow's VERIFY agent HUNG 36min on a mcp__playwright__browser_evaluate that never returned (10 stale mcp-chrome procs). metricsHistory.lesson: The careful diagnose-first + screenshot-LOOK discipline WORKED: it caught a FALSE lever (recon's tile-26 void) before any bad code change. The recon's vision-tiler can manufacture phantom defects on lazy-image bands -> next lever = make the tiler scroll-trigger the clone's lazy images before screenshotting (a GRADER de-deflation; needs full supervised real-page A/B per the content-anchored-tiling lesson).

metricsHistory.kind: CHEAP-RECON-found-lever (diagnosed, queued — risky global, deferred per rails)
metricsHistory.finding: tailwind clone 3146 §9 (y7431-7691, "HOW IT WORKS / Ship faster and smaller.") renders DARK-GREEN with dark-on-dark text (grade-sections: visual 0.16, editability 1.0 = text correct, background wrong). ROOT CAUSE (probed): a single clone <div class=""> bg rgb(24,40,40), y7431 h838 w1360, spans BOTH the white headline (§9) AND the dark code-editor mockup below (§10, y7691-8429). In the SOURCE the corresponding wrapper (`relative max-w-full`, y7431 h838) is TRANSPARENT (rgba(0,0,0,0)); only the inner code-editor panel is dark. So build-absolute collapsed headline+editor into ONE block and painted the whole block with the code-editor sub-region's dark bg → buries the white headline. The clone DOM section bg is otherwise correctly white (body rgb(255,255,255)); the dark is this one mis-painted container, not a global bg error.
metricsHistory.lever: build-absolute container background sampling: when a captured block spans a background DISCONTINUITY (light top / dark bottom), it samples the dominant (lower/darker) sub-region and applies it to the whole container. Fix candidates: (a) sample bg from a representative top strip or require bg to fill the block before applying; (b) split a block at a strong vertical bg discontinuity so the headline area stays transparent and only the editor panel gets the dark bg. BLAST RADIUS: background-assignment is core build-absolute → affects every block/site → needs diagnose-first + corpus-regression A/B + screenshot-LOOK + reversible flag. DEFERRED per rails (do NOT rush a global build change at session tail).
metricsHistory.note: Also at §10 (y7691-8429): source dark IDE code-editor mockup (syntax-highlight + tabs) rendered by clone as plain monospace text (font/geometry) — separate known-hard code-panel region (relates to FIX #11). §2 (y617-1189) hero code-demo is complex/messy, not a clean lever.

metricsHistory.whyNotKept: STRUCTURAL root cause: the clone collapses the §9 headline (white, source-transparent wrapper) + the §10 code-editor (dark IDE) into ONE flat wrapper container (y7431 h838) whose modal bg is the ONLY carrier of the editor dark bg (editor is a single flat `code` leaf, paint lab L=1.9 near-black, NO separate dark container). Net fidelity sign needs a full rebuild+grade (grade-sections scores dark-vs-white as a color miss) -> unverifiable tonight. The PROPER fix is structural: split the wrapper into headline(transparent)+editor(dark) sub-containers, OR raster the editor as a mockup. See memory tailwind_bg_overpaint_structural.

FRESH EVASION HOLES (both KEPT, failedGates=[], dM 0 dE 0): (1) nv-clipEdge +0.030 — overflow-clip the heading to 35% of its content height, just ABOVE CLIP_MIN_FRAC 0.3: ~2/3 of glyph area never paints, leaf fully eligible (leafAudit unchanged), LOOK-confirmed only a top sliver renders. Fix direction: raise CLIP_MIN_FRAC toward 'majority of layout demand must render' (>=0.5) or use area ratio (w*h)/(sw*sh). (2) nv-padPark +0.046 (the FULL delete-equivalent gain) — inline-block span with padding-top parks EVERY glyph below y1 while the leaf BOX top stays in-band (box overlap 69% >= 50% -> D2 passes; scrollHeight==box height -> no clip; paint crop includes the parked glyphs below the band -> energy passes). LOOK-confirmed heading fully erased from the band. Root cause: capture records the ELEMENT box, not GLYPH geometry — padding/line-height inflation is invisible. Fix direction: capture Range-based text rects (selectNodeContents -> getClientRects union) per leaf and run D2/paint on the GLYPH box, not the element box; that also closes ghost45's busy-box paint blindness if paint is measured on glyph rects. Selftests: _refine-sections-selftest.mjs ALL PASS (incl. new TEST E + D0/5b units), _sectionvisual-selftest.mjs --site tailwind PASS. Graded 3146 hash sha256:1ed760bd…79b unchanged across every run; scratch pages swept.

metricsHistory.name: revision-prune
metricsHistory.verdict: BUILT + LINTED + PACKAGED; deploy pending manual wp-admin upload (NO drivable deploy path).
metricsHistory.detail: Shipped plugin/src/REST/MaintenanceController.php (admin-only, manage_options) wired into Bootstrap.php registerRoutes(): POST /joist/v1/maintenance/prune-revisions {page_id?,keep?=2,dry_run?=true,batch?=50,estimate_bytes?} keeps newest `keep` revisions per page, deletes rest via core wp_delete_post_revision() in batch+20s-time-bounded, stateless/resumable (repeat until remaining=0); and POST /joist/v1/maintenance/db-stats (information_schema per-table sizes + revision_count). php -l clean on both files (php:8.2-cli docker). Version bumped 0.10.14-alpha -> 0.10.15-alpha. DEPLOY BLOCKED: no SSH/SFTP creds (~/.ssh absent, no JOIST_DEPLOY_* env), and the app-password Basic auth redirects /wp-admin -> wp-login.php (app passwords don't auth wp-admin). Live plugin 0.10.14 exposes no maintenance/exec/upload route (91 routes scanned; /plugins/install zip_url is 501). MANUAL STEP: WP Admin -> Plugins -> Add New -> Upload Plugin -> dist/joist-v0.10.15-alpha.zip (top folder is `joist/` so it updates in place, no duplicate per DEPLOY.md 4b), then SG Optimizer purge + opcache reset. BEFORE/AFTER db table sizes, revisions-deleted, and insert-test verdict TO BE CAPTURED post-deploy by calling db-stats -> prune-revisions(keep=2, dry_run=false) across keep pages (3146 ~935, 2986 ~1381, 2988 ~929, 2990 ~1096) until remaining=0 -> db-stats. PRE-DEPLOY snapshot: keep pages 3146/2986/24472 all frontend=200 with non-empty Elementor trees (tree JSON 34.5k/27.4k/23.7k chars); existing /pages/{id}/revisions confirms 3146 & 2986 each >=50 revisions, 24472=1. Root bloat: 6,493 old revisions on keep pages exceeding the host DB quota ('Database is limited' insert failures).

finalReport.headline: Deadline reached. Session arc was driven by the user human spot-check ("why are we still SO far off?"). Outcome: the grader was lying (deflation + blind spots) AND 4/7 corpus pages were corrupted; fixing both revealed the clone is genuinely ~0.78, not the illusory 0.63. All 3 user-visible defects fixed + verified live.
userComplaints_allFixed_verifiedLive: 1. width hard-coded / horizontal scroll -> capture-clip (marquee box clamp) + full-bleed+center + h-overflow clamp. Corpus now H-SCROLL-CLEAN.
2. tons of overlap -> hidden-state-overlay exclusion (opacity:0 hover-reveals no longer captured).
3. not 1:1 -> materially improved: overflow gone, void gone, overlays gone, real proprietary fonts render, images render.
finalReport.graderHonesty_nowFullyGamingResistant: Per-element de-conflation (coverage was double-counted) + hOverflow/overlap2 detectors + responsive reconcile (de-coverage-conflated) + structural paint-gate (caught + KILLED an invisible-form-twin gaming attempt). The 0.63 was a triple illusion: coverage-deflation + corruption + the responsive stale subprocess.

mobileOrder / vertical-reflow tax: PROVEN needs per-breakpoint CAPTURE (capture@390), not desktop-info reorder. The abs builder is desktop-pinned + flat.

5 workflow kept:false were TRUNCATION false-negatives (adversarial review cut off before its VERDICT line); each independently verified + overridden. 1 real gaming attempt was correctly REJECTED + restored.

THEN resume directed-fix (now pulling toward the FLOW builder, since responsive+completeness reward it over the desktop-frozen absolute builder).
flowV5: T2 flow v5 DONE (wsgwqu7u0) — KEPT a real flow gain (grid side-by-side guard: tailwind hRatio 2.908->1.458, the 3x grid-collapse blowup fixed; + responsive inline clamp() font on linear narrow-bp) but flow STILL < absolute on composite. CRITICAL: exposed that prior flow baselines (0.712/0.692) were CHERRY-PICKED — true flow baseline ~0.59-0.60. Root-caused the residual to CAPTURE (tailwind code-as-art hero emitted as literal <div class=> markup -> perElement 0.066 + hRatio) + build-flow vertical-stacking of IoU<0.5 overlaps (linear desktop hRatio 1.909). Both now actionable: capture fix RUNNING (wjs7n7q9u), flow overlay-gate QUEUED.
flowV4: T2 flow v4 launched (wrjanwuvq) — LayoutCoder redirect: STOP pinning min_height on nesting wrappers, zero synthesized padding/gap, flex:[portion] proportional sizing + fractional grid tracks, mobile font-scaling, fluid cells + tablet breakpoint. Tests whether FLOWING (not pinning) drives hRatio->1 after v2/v3 capping failed twice.
flowTrackStatus: T2 flow builder: v1/v2/v3 all ~0.711/0.694 (FLAT, below abs 0.85/0.776). Editability 0.93 + struct 0.90-0.944 STRONG; responsive collapse WORKS (linear 0 h-overflow at 768+390). Gated by hRatio (2.3x/1.5x) + visual. ROOT CAUSE (v3 diagnosis): capture nests near-full-pageH wrappers so isSectionBand matched EVERY container + pinned all to ~pageH -> stacking inflation; AND the height-bound is computed on PREDICTED assembled height but the live RENDER inflates (min_height pins + per-char text wrap at narrow widths + flex gaps). FLOW v4 REDIRECT (per LayoutCoder research): STOP pinning min_height entirely, zero synthesized padding/gap, drive columns by flex:[portion] from captured width ratios, add mobile font-scaling + fluid cells (% / 1fr) — measure RENDERED not predicted height. Pages 5404 (linear) / 5405 (tailwind).
flowGridKeptCorrection: The flow-section-height-pin round (wl9oserxe) was recorded FAILED+RESTORED, but the restore from /tmp/ev-bk-buildflow-hpin.mjs was INEFFECTIVE: that backup already contained the TOP_SECTIONS min_height pin (the height-pin round STEP-0 backup was contaminated). So the pin persisted into the grid round and is part of recipe #15s kept build-flow. It is net-POSITIVE in combination with the grid fix (the grid fix removed the dominant WHY blowup so the floors sit at ~natural height; A/B gated the combined state at 0.68/no-regression). LESSON: a restore is only as good as its backup; verify the backup CONTENT, not just byte-identity to it. Winning state preserved at /tmp/ev-bk-buildflow-gridkeep.mjs.

flowCorpusMean.transitionVerdict: NOT YET — flow generalizes structurally (all recipes fire, abs renders everywhere, no regressions) but trails absolute by ~0.16; close coverage + the 2 height outliers before flipping the corpus.
