gridAutoRowsKsesStripped: DURABLE FINDING (flow-grid-align-items): grid_auto_rows is INERT in flow output — Elementor container-grid renders grid-auto-rows:auto regardless, and the joist PUT/kses strips the px value. Do NOT rely on grid_auto_rows for height capping; bound at the CHILD/cell level (max-height/overflow) instead. align-items:start DOES land + is correct (cards take content height) but was composite-flat because the dominant inflation is overflowing tall children + footer column-stacking, not row-equalization.
coverageGapTruth: CORRECTION (flow-coverage-diagnostic w59h5naib): the flow areaCoverage gap is NOT missing imagery / region-capture. It is (1) tailwind = capture-side MISCLASSIFICATION (hero prose captured as kind:code + class-name pollution -> garbled <pre> + ~750px cascade drift de-aligning everything) and (2) framer = build-side SUBTREE DROP (display:contents 0x0 wrapper drops 88% of the page via visibleKid). Both have all nodes in the capture. region-capture imagery is deprioritized (imagery is already captured + emitted; it just drifts or is dropped). barClassTokenPoison + this converge: tailwind needs the prose-not-code + strip-class-tokens capture fix.
graderCoverageUndercountFlag: SUPERVISED grader-honesty item (from flow-visiblekid-flatten): framer flow clone renders 420 elementor-elements but perElement areaCoverage reads 0.0556; tailwind capture has 146 leaves vs 514 DOM-visible texts. The grader CLONE-SIDE capture may under-count rendered widgets, deflating areaCoverage -> capping every flow composite even when the clone is good (framer recovered 316 widgets but composite only +0.087). Investigate as a supervised grader fix (self-test=1.0, reversible, A/B): does the clone-capture resolve all rendered widgets? If under-counting, fixing it lifts coverage across ALL 7 sites at once. Do NOT act blindly — high blast radius (every verdict).
objectiveNow_deepflatten: GRADER UPDATE (adopted grader-deep-flatten): areaCoverage now uses deep-flatten on the grader capture (page-spanning wrappers bypass the 40-leaf cap, source+clone symmetric), correcting a confirmed false-deflation that crushed deep-DOM clone coverage. Reversible GRADER_NO_DEEP_FLATTEN=1 -> exact prior. self-test=1.0 both modes. Builders untouched (capture-ensemble capped). This RE-BASELINES deep-DOM (mostly flow) clone composites HONESTLY upward; shallow clones (absolute, tailwind) ~unchanged. RESIDUAL: ~half the deep-clone leaves still unresolved (follow-up grader item) — the metric is truer but not yet complete.

flowCorpusMeanHonest.dominantLevers: hRatio (supabase 1.778, framer 1.568, vercel 1.474) + responsive (0.32-0.47 cross-cutting) + raw visual/ssim — NOT coverage
flowCorpusMeanHonest.note: post BOTH grader-honesty fixes (deep-flatten + ydrift): supabase ~0.674 (ydrift +0.048), mean ~0.603; ydrift mainly lifts complete-but-stretched clones (supabase), incomplete (framer/linear) unaffected

perElementMatcherFlag: HIGH-VALUE grader-honesty lever (from recipe #19 / w7279427x): supabase ssim 0.897 + struct 1.0 but perElement color 0.048 / text 0.087 — a CONTRADICTION (ssim says 90% pixel-match -> colors ARE right). The perElement Hungarian matcher matches source<->clone by POSITION; when the clone is uniformly stretched (hRatio>1) the y-positions shift and matching fails -> color/typo/text (position-INDEPENDENT content properties) cannot be credited. So y-drift is DOUBLE-PENALIZED (hits position AND breaks color/typo/text matching). Honest decomposition: penalize y-drift in the POSITION sub-score + hRatio/responsive (already done), but measure color/typo/text on CONTENT-matched pairs tolerant of uniform y-scale. Cross-cutting: starves perElement on supabase 1.486/framer 1.568/vercel 1.474/linear 1.137. DIAGNOSE first (read-only): does y-normalizing the clone capture make color/text jump? If yes -> supervised perElement matcher fix (self-test=1.0 + reversible). Risk: do not over-credit a genuinely mis-positioned clone — only tolerate UNIFORM y-scale.
linearRasterizesTextFlag: CLONER BUG (from perelement-ydrift-diagnostic): the linear FLOW clone has 33 images vs source 8 and ~1 comparable text node vs source 232 (only 33.9% of source text present exactly) -> it RASTERIZED ~half the page into image bitmaps, violating the words-must-be-rebuilt rule + tanking its honest perElement completeness. Investigate where build-flow/capture rasterizes text on linear (likely the dark app-mockup / overlay regions captured as image leaves instead of decomposed text). Separate from the matcher fix; a real cloner-quality lever.
graderState: GRADER now = ydrift-only (deep-flatten REVERTED as net-zero+inconsistent). perElement uniform-y-scale matcher (ydrift, anti-game-verified, reversible GRADER_NO_YSCALE=1) is the one adopted grader-honesty fix. self-test composite=1.0. deep-flatten removed from capture-layout + grade-sections (pre-deep-flatten backups restored); a clone-aware deep-flatten redesign is future work. ksesResponsiveCorrection: CORRECTION (abs-responsive-diagnostic w1igms3fo, empirically verified): per-breakpoint _tablet/_mobile keys are NOT kses-stripped (they round-trip intact) — my earlier overlay-round belief was WRONG. The real reason absolute is desktop-frozen: _position is not a RESPONSIVE Elementor control, so _position_mobile is stored but never compiles to CSS (.elementor-absolute welds position:absolute at all widths). The working responsive path = page custom_css media query (Pro channel, same as @font-face) that un-pins .elementor-absolute below 1024.

researchCadencePolicy: USER 2026-06-04: fold EXTERNAL research in aggressively — fire research-refuel.workflow.js (web fan-out, FRONTIER-grounded) every ~3 rounds, NOT only on deep plateau. DRIVER RULE (heartbeat step 4, pick-next-round): if roundsSinceResearch >= researchEvery(3) -> next round = research-refuel (refresh its FRONTIER const from the current open walls A-E + kept/rejected scorecard first), then set roundsSinceResearch=0; else pick a normal cloner/grader round + roundsSinceResearch+=1. Research output: fold autonomousSafe directives into directedFixBacklog (gated rounds) + supervised items into supervisedQueue + topInsight into the journal. Research runs read-only (no cloner edits) so it can also overlap a build round if desired, but default = one heavy round at a time per the rails.

authStackBug.problem: /tmp/joist-auth.env (sourced by every verify workflow HARD rule) was MISSING; the only present file /tmp/joist-auth-1.env overrides JOIST_BASE=http://localhost:8001 (Elementor 3.28.4). Verify agents SPLIT stacks: resend+supabase reached the real corpus stack (sg-host, Elementor 4.0.9) and graded correctly (0.581/0.634); framer+tailwind fell to localhost-3.28.4 -> PUT 422 schema.invalid_settings (text-editor has no control _flex_grow on 3.28.4) -> blank pages -> floor grades 0.043/0.071 (GARBAGE). Gate decisions were being made on mixed real+broken data.
authStackBug.fix: Wrote canonical /tmp/joist-auth.env: JOIST_BASE=https://georges232.sg-host.com + JOIST_AUTH_B64 from the joist-georges232 MCP config Authorization header (sg-host Basic auth). PROBED: wp/v2/pages/2988, joist/v1/pages/2988, /wp-json all 200; namespaces show elementor/v1 + elementor-pro/v1 + joist/v1 (real 4.0.9+Pro stack). Builders/grader default JOIST_BASE to sg-host so a present+correct /tmp/joist-auth.env makes every agent hit the SAME real stack.
authStackBug.guard: FUTURE verify workflows MUST assert after sourcing: $JOIST_BASE == https://georges232.sg-host.com (fail-fast else). Do NOT source /tmp/joist-auth-1.env for corpus grading (localhost variant).
authStackBug.caution: framer (2990) + tailwind in the LATEST verify run returned artifact grades — any corpus number from a localhost-3.28.4 build is invalid; re-grade on sg-host.

flexGrowPortability.fact: build-flow/build-absolute emit _flex_grow on text-editor widgets. Elementor 4.0.9 (sg-host, real corpus stack) ACCEPTS it; Elementor 3.28.4 (localhost) REJECTS the WHOLE save with 422 schema.invalid_settings (text-editor has no control named _flex_grow) -> blank page. The product is silently coupled to Elementor 4.x flex-container controls.
flexGrowPortability.impact: Not blocking the flywheel (corpus = sg-host 4.0.9). BUT a real product-portability bug: on any Elementor 3.28.x customer site every build would produce a blank page. Queue for product hardening: version-probe + omit/guard 4.x-only controls on older Elementor.

responsiveFrontierTruth.flowSolved: build-flow ALREADY reflows multi-card/logo grids 3->2->1 via emitted grid_columns_grid_tablet + grid_columns_grid_mobile per-breakpoint overrides (verified clean single-column at 390, zero horizontal overflow on tailwind+vercel+supabase).
responsiveFrontierTruth.ramVerdict: RAM auto-fit minmax is REDUNDANT with the above + breaks the desktop-invariant on uneven-cell grids (auto-fit picks a different N). Do NOT re-propose auto-fit responsive grids.
responsiveFrontierTruth.openWall: The responsive gap is the ABS builder: recipe #20 abs-responsive-unpin does only a CRUDE everything->1col collapse at narrow widths (no 3->2->1 card-row regrouping). ABS is the router primary (0.648 vs flow 0.616), so its responsive term caps the router. LEVER: detect card/comparable-width rows in the abs tree + apply flows per-breakpoint grid-override reflow at tablet/mobile instead of the blanket 1col un-pin.

absMobileOverflowBug.bug: build-absolute pins the editable full-width header <nav> + <footer> chrome at the SOURCE css width:1440px and NEVER un-pins them responsively. Result: docW=1440 at the 390 viewport on EVERY abs clone -> horizontal scroll + content clipped off the left edge at mobile. A glaring, human-obvious mobile defect on all absolute clones; it (not the card-rows) is why the abs-responsive port measured reflows=false.
absMobileOverflowBug.fix: un-pin the nav/footer (and any fixed-width full-bleed chrome) to width:100% / responsive at <=1024 via the same scoped @media custom_css channel recipe #20 already uses for the body un-pin, OR emit them at width:100% from the start. Render test: docW MUST == viewport at 390 (no horizontal scroll); nav/footer full-width-responsive; desktop identical.
absMobileOverflowBug.leverage: HIGH + BROAD — affects mobile fidelity on EVERY abs clone (router primary). Raises the responsive baseline across the corpus. Independent of the card-row reflow. Likely-keep (clear bug, clear test). Also UNBLOCKS a clean re-test of the grid card-row reflow port next.

absResponsiveMechanism.gridWorks: grid_columns_grid + _tablet/_mobile breakpoint overrides DO compile + reflow 3/2/1 on abs pages (proven on scratch pages 2990/6724/6731).
absResponsiveMechanism.flexFails: _element_custom_width does NOT size flex container children (only abs widgets) -> do not use flex-width% for card reflow.
absResponsiveMechanism.caveats: reflowing card-row must be excluded from the <=1024 un-pin selector; release cell min_height at mobile; preserve the abs band y/height so desktop flow is unchanged; un-pin cell-relative leaf left-offsets at mobile.
absResponsiveMechanism.openGraderQuestion: the ports respOn DROPPED vs the crude 1col un-pin (0.4377->0.3203 supabase). Likely because the reflow rendered badly (min_height/bleed/overflow), but VERIFY whether the RLG responsive metric genuinely under-rewards true 3->2->1 vs clean 1col once a CLEAN reflow exists. If so, that is a responsive-grader honesty bug.

graderResponsiveDeflationBug.bug1_RLG: DISPROVEN — RLG correctly orders true-reflow (1.0) above crude-1col (0.824); credits the 2-col tablet step. Real-clone reflow drops are REAL narrow-width differences (builder), not RLG bugs.
graderResponsiveDeflationBug.bug2_perElementNesting: FIXED in perelement-score.mjs (struct-invariant: drop visually-invisible wrappers). MIRROR into grade-responsive PROBE_FN still PENDING -> responsive channel half-fixed.
graderResponsiveDeflationBug.impact: BOTH false-deflate real responsive improvements -> the flywheel has been UNABLE to show responsive gains (3 responsive rounds all scored flat/down despite human-better output). Fixing this unblocks wall B entirely. HIGHEST grader-honesty priority.
graderResponsiveDeflationBug.status: RESOLVED — per-element struct-invariant KEPT + responsive mobile-prop term KEPT (recipe #24, coverage-weighted, deterministic self-test). Responsive now honestly measures mobile overflow+height.

graderResponsiveSelftestFlaky.fact: grade-responsive --selftest re-captures the source url TWICE independently and compares; live re-capture variance (lazy content, marquees, animations) makes RLG node-matching score 0.957-0.999, occasionally below the EPS=0.04 (>=0.96) rail. Pre-existing; surfaced by mobile-prop v2.
graderResponsiveSelftestFlaky.impact: Blocks deterministic responsive-grader additions (they pass on their own but the RLG base flakes the rail). Does NOT corrupt real source-vs-CLONE grading, only the self-consistency assertion.
graderResponsiveSelftestFlaky.fix: PREREQUISITE for mobile-prop: either (a) self-test compares ONE capture to a deep-copy of itself (truly deterministic), or (b) widen the self-test EPS to ~0.08 (>=0.92) with justification. Then re-land mobile-prop (coverage-weighted, v2 logic).
graderResponsiveSelftestFlaky.status: FIXED — SELFTEST clone = deepcopy of source (deterministic EXACTLY 1.0 x5).

corpusMeanHonest.grader: grade-sections current (all recipes #20-27 + fragment-merge), build-absolute, median-of-3, CONSISTENT abs state
corpusMeanHonest.note: TRUSTWORTHY consistent-state baseline. responsive CAPPED on abs; the addressable lever is dynamic-content-emit (struct floor). grades reliable (spread 0.006) except tailwind chunkedMediaCount oscillation.

topDefectRanked.1: responsive VERTICAL-reflow — abs widgets keep desktop top/height when un-pinned -> mobile docH 2.1x-9.5x source -> near-empty column. THE lever (7/8 sites, responsive 0.3625 floor). Fix recipe #20 to reset top/height at <=1024 so widgets compact into a natural mobile stack.
topDefectRanked.2: structural-capture-fidelity (dynamic-content-not-captured) — live React demos / animated / canvas sections lost (reactdev/resend/framer); structural 0.4973 floor.
topDefectRanked.3: editability-quality — strongest dimension (0.855), lowest leverage, do not chase.

joistCheckAQuirk.fact: Joist Document::save() Check A on Elementor 4.0.9 returns 422 atomic_save_silent_failure when the element TREE is byte-identical to the live page (e.g. a custom_css-only change). Workaround: write an empty/reset tree first, then the real tree (a real tree-change passes Check A). Affects any page_settings-only update.
joistCheckAQuirk.impact: medium — A/B rounds that toggle a custom_css flag must reset the tree between builds or the second save 422s.

absMobileBgVanishRegression.bug: recipe #20/#23 un-pin collapses decorative bg-rects to height 0 at <=1024 -> section BACKGROUND colors/images VANISH at mobile (supabase dark hero/sections render WHITE at 390 in both ON+OFF). Human-visible mobile fidelity regression; the grader (mobile-prop measures docH/overflow, SSIM is desktop) does NOT catch it.
absMobileBgVanishRegression.fix: on emit, set each section bg-rect color as background_color on the nearest CONTENT container (so the container carries its own bg); at <=1024 the bg-rect collapses harmlessly + the container bg shows. Must be desktop-identical (redundant bg behind content at desktop). Dedicated round.
absMobileBgVanishRegression.status: known — fix queued (separate round; bg-correctness, human-visible, ungraded)

absMobileResidualTruth: After vreflow #23: mobile-docH residual is DOMINANTLY un-pinned IMAGES rendering full-width at natural aspect (framer 46%/supabase 27%), then retained text heights (~20%), 12px reflow margins (9-13%), card-row grid heights. Image mobile-width constraint (keep captured width) is the biggest lever. This is the abs-builders fundamental desktop-pixel->mobile-stack limitation; full 1:1 mobile compaction is hard.

absMobileCeiling.finding: The abs-builder has an ARCHITECTURAL mobile ceiling: vreflow #23 crushed mobile docH from 2-9.5x to 0.95x(tailwind)/3.1x(supabase)/4.76x(framer), but the RESIDUAL (un-pinned images full-width at natural aspect = framer 46%, retained text heights ~20%, 12px reflow margins, 1-col image stacking vs source dense rows) is fundamental to flat desktop-pixel to mobile-stack. Further squeezing = diminishing returns + desktop-leak risk (vreflow-residual broke tailwind desktop). Full mobile 1:1 needs the FLOW builder or the structure-inference rework (wave-4 #2). DECISION: responsive given its 2-3 rounds (big keep #23 + thorough diagnosis); PIVOT to the #2 corpus lever = STRUCTURAL floor 0.4973 (dynamic-content-not-captured).

structuralGapTruth.finding: The struct floor (reactdev 0.34, resend 0.337, framer 0.383) is NOT click-gated content. It is: (a) canvas / iframe(Sandpack) / live-React-output PREVIEW surfaces that paint visually but have no/sparse walkable DOM -> dropped -> sparse failing sections; (b) decorative-SVG counted/missed as content. LEVER: surface region-raster (element-level image per cohesive visual surface, blank-raster-guarded, GPU flags for canvas) + SVG-vs-content classification. NOT click-to-reveal (proven dead).

surfaceRasterWordBuryingConcern.concern: surface-raster (recipe #25) dropped resend struct 0.444->0.302 and rastered the hero heading region -> may bury words in images, violating the user principle (words rebuilt, images OK). MUST verify + tighten: raster ONLY text-free visual surfaces; preserve overlaid text as native leaves.
surfaceRasterWordBuryingConcern.status: RESOLVED (recipe #26 word-safe tighten): gate counts the element's own text + bails on headings -> the hero H1 is native text again; text-free surfaces still raster.

threeRoundPlan.1: flow-router: re-baseline build-FLOW on the honest grader + best-per-site router (flow cracks responsive where abs is capped; linear flow 0.607 vs abs 0.378). Biggest UNCAPPED lever for the responsive floor.
threeRoundPlan.2: dynamic-content-not-captured (capture-side: imagery region-capture + structured-code-block capture) — broadest addressable, 4 sites, struct+visual.
threeRoundPlan.3: text-collision de-dupe in build-absolute (USER feedback #4, supabase hero overlap + nav/CTA dup; cheap flagship-restore).

flowRouterFinding.finding: Router (best-per-site abs vs flow) gains only ~+0.005 (flow wins 2/7: supabase, linear). Flow responsive 0.3129 > abs 0.2296 but flow visual/structural lower -> net flow<=abs. Do NOT adopt route-to-flow as a corpus lever. The responsive floor needs a hybrid (abs fidelity + flow reflow) or structure-inference, not routing. abs stays primary.

gateDesignLesson.lesson: Gate on what the grader ACTUALLY measures for the defect class. The text-collision-dedupe fix (removes user-#4 doubled labels) is human-visible but the grader's collisionRate skips same-text overlaps by design -> gating on collisionRate-drop wrongly reverted a correct fix. For human-visible-but-ungraded fixes, gate on the human-visible criterion (dupLabelsGone) + composite-no-reg + self-test, NOT a metric that cannot move.

coverageCrushConfirmed.finding: PROVEN (reactdev /tmp/pe-gsec-reactdev): the per-element score multiplies EVERY sub-score (color/typo/pos/text/effects) by areaCoverage, so on incomplete clones (low coverage) accurate sub-scores are FALSE-DEFLATED. reactdev raw color 0.8276 -> reported 0.16 via x0.1931. The grader conflates ACCURACY (high) with COMPLETENESS (low: 82/205 matched). The corpus is MORE accurate than the composite suggests; the gap is COMPLETENESS. Fix = #6 grader re-attribution (separate coverage dim + raw per-pair sub-scores), keeping coverage as its own penalized dimension (anti-gaming).

completenessIsTheLever.finding: CONFIRMED across #6: per-element ACCURACY is high (~0.85); the composite gap is COMPLETENESS (coverage). The grader's accuracy x coverage IS honest overall-fidelity (do NOT un-gate — gameable). To raise the composite: RAISE COVERAGE (match more of the source's real content), which the multiply amplifies. Two sub-levers: (a) capture genuinely-missing content (dynamic imagery/sections), (b) reduce OVER-COUNTED source nodes (decorative SVG/fragments inflating srcNodes) via fragment/section merge. Diagnose which dominates per site.

measurementIntegrityBug.finding: Cross-round corpus numbers are NOT comparable because (a) flow-router-rebaseline overwrote all 7 pages with FLOW builds and only some were rebuilt abs since -> a grade reads whatever builder last wrote the page; (b) grade-responsive RLG re-captures live + varies. PROOF: #6 graded reactdev at coverage 0.19/composite 0.53 (a FLOW leftover) while a fresh abs rebuild graded 0.96/0.616. FIX: a definitive clean re-baseline (rebuild ALL 7 with build-absolute, all recipes live, MULTI-PASS grade averaged) to reset to a known consistent state + trustworthy mean. Going forward, always rebuild-then-grade in the SAME round (never grade a page another round may have overwritten).

chunkedMediaNondeterminism.finding: tailwind grade spread 0.039 (vs ~0.001 elsewhere) traces to the chunked-media detector's chunkedMediaCount oscillating 2<->4 across identical-page captures -> struct 0.612<->0.756. Minor (only tailwind), but the chunked-media detector is capture-variance-sensitive. Low priority; median-of-3 absorbs it.

gateCalibrationLesson2.lesson: 2nd mis-calibrated gate this session (1st: text-collision v1 gated on an unmovable metric). dynamic-content-emit gated on structOn>structOff+0.02; the fix raised composite +0.042 + edit +0.07 + coverage +0.05 + bands-native + no-reg + self-test 1.0 but struct rose only 0.019 -> auto-restored a clear win. RULE: gate keeps on the HEADLINE objective (composite-up OR the user-principle the round serves) + no-reg + self-test; never let an arbitrary sub-score threshold veto a composite win.

directions: 1. REFINE-TO-1:1 inner loop (per-clone render->diff->repair->re-render until pixel-matched, then distill repairs into recipes) — raises the ceiling on every site; the biggest flywheel upgrade. refine.mjs exists (tasks #16/#25 completed) but likely stale vs the current abs pipeline + honest grader -> assess+rewire+prove+extend.

2. CAPTURE RESPONSIVE (multi-viewport 390/768/1440 capture + hybrid grid reflow) — the only path off the capped responsive floor (0.258); 1:1 requires matching the source's ACTUAL mobile layout we don't currently capture.

3. CORPUS BREADTH (20-archetype taxonomy: ecommerce/blog/portfolio/docs/dashboard/agency/restaurant) + DETERMINISTIC SYNTHETIC BENCH (hero/card-grid/nav/pricing/footer ground-truth) — makes wins generalize + kills measurement variance (half this session's reverts).

4. EDITABILITY QUALITY (global colors/fonts via Elementor variables, semantic nesting, a real editability metric) + MOTION capture (scroll/hover/parallax via the scoped GSAP/Pro-Motion escape hatch) — the weakest halves of '1:1 + editable the way a human dev would'.
META: parallelize the flywheel into worktree tracks (grader/capture/build/research touch different files) to 3-4x throughput.
phasePlan_2026_06_05.sequence: Start #1 (refine-to-1:1, highest ceiling-raise) + #3 (bench, for trustworthy generalization), then #2 (responsive), then #4. User selected ALL FOUR.

refineEngineBuilt.fact: refine-clone.mjs (per-clone refine driver) is built + proven: grade -> /tmp/pe-pairs per-pair deltas -> resolve widget id by text-Dice+box -> joist POST /pages/{id}/patch {ops:[{op:update_settings,element_id,settings}],expected_hash} -> re-grade -> keep-if-up (KEEP_EPS 0.004) -> clean revert. Live 5-widget/iter patching is below-noise; the engine is for LIVE position-nudge later. The per-pair repair-map output (perelement-score --emit-pairs / REFINE_PAIRS=1 -> /tmp/pe-pairs-<tag>.json) is additive, self-test 1.0.

buildTimeAccuracyRedirect.finding: The scalable per-element lever is BUILD-TIME accuracy snapping across ALL widgets, not live patches. Priority: (1) FONT-FAMILY assignment (typo capped 0.46 until source family matches — family=50% of typo) — assign captured typography_font_family per widget + ensure @font-face registered + inline on text-editor/html inner divs; (2) color-snap matched text to source color; (3) font-size-snap to source px. All in build-absolute, corpus-wide, reversible, corpus-gated.

coverageThrottleResolved.finding: areaCoverage (~0.49 supabase) is NOT missing content — it is GRADER 1:1-MATCHER STARVATION of co-located stacked reproduced nodes (96.9% of unmatched-src area has the content rendered at the same box; 0% dropped). The composite ~0.56 UNDERSTATES true fidelity. HONEST FIX = symmetric container-coalesce pre-pass in perelement-score (collapse stacked same-box wrapper containers -> content leaf on both sides; self-test 1.0; anti-gaming incomplete-clone test). This is the biggest honest composite lever left + serves user-#1 (grader honesty). Builder content-recovery has ~0% headroom on the corpus.

contaminatedBackupRecovery.event: matcher-coalesce v1 gate REVERTED, but the auto-restore was CONTAMINATED: the workflow restored /tmp/ev-bk-perelem-coalesce.mjs which ITSELF contained coalesce (33 refs) because the impl made its STEP-0 backup AFTER editing -> the GAMEABLE v1 coalesce was left LIVE + default-ON as the active grader. CAUGHT by grep-ing the file content (not trusting the verdict string). RECOVERY: restored from /tmp/ev-bk-perelem-covsep.mjs (verified coalesce=0, blockMerge=4, struct-invariant=3 — all kept recipes intact), self-test PASS composite 1.0, saved /tmp/ev-TRUSTED-perelem-clean.mjs (content-verified). Lost only the refine emit-pairs telemetry (opt-in, not default grader).
contaminatedBackupRecovery.lesson: RE-CONFIRMS the banked lesson: VERIFY backup CONTENT (grep for the change), never trust byte-identity or a workflow restore. Point restores at a TRUSTED content-verified backup, not a self-made STEP-0 cp.

coverageThreadConcluded.conclusion: areaCoverage ~0.49 is matcher-starvation of REPRODUCED stacked content (not missing); composite under-counts true fidelity but crediting it is fundamentally gameable (coalesce v1+v2 both fail anti-gaming). The grader is an HONEST CONSERVATIVE under-count (safe direction). LEAVE IT. The cloner is better than the number. Stop grader rounds; pivot to bench + responsive. The matcher-coalesce.workflow.js is preserved if a future per-region-match-verified approach is wanted (supervised, hard).

benchLive.fact: bench/bench-run.mjs is the new STANDING deterministic regression suite (6 ground-truth blocks, spread 0). Run it to A/B any builder/capture/grader change variance-free + catch regressions vs bench/baseline.json. Isolated builder bugs: text-coverage collapse on hero/pricing (0.32) + abs builder fidelity ceiling 0.858 on pixel-exact input.
benchLive.status: live, use for all future A/B

benchOverfitLesson.lesson: A fix validated ONLY on the synthetic bench can OVERFIT it (bench-text-coverage #29 raised bench hero coverage 0.32->0.998 but DROPPED live corpus composite 0.5643->0.5399 via visual -0.076 — real-site bgRects carry background visual the synthetic blocks lack). RULE: bench is for DETERMINISM + ISOLATION; every bench-validated BUILDER fix must ALSO pass a LIVE-corpus gate (>=2 live sites, composite no-reg) before keeping. Add real-background blocks to the bench to close the gap.

bgrectProbeFix.finding: THE clean coverage lever: full-bleed gradient/image section-background bgRects emitted as childless divs w/ an 8px probe get mis-captured as phantom mockups (unmatched-clone area -> areaCoverage crush, hero 0.32). FIX = >=24px probe child on FULL-BLEED section bgRects only -> capture sees a container that matches the source band. LIVE-VERIFIED: areaCoverage 0.3225->0.9976, hero visual 0.607->0.886, SSIM unchanged. Additive (never removes a panel) -> live-safe (unlike #29s suppression arm). Ship probe-arm alone, scoped to full-bleed bands.

breadthFindings.topNewLever: build-absolute mis-classifies a dense repeated-row content list (HN 195 rows) as a nav-menu -> structural=0 + collisions. Generalizes to ecommerce grids/search-results/comment-threads/changelogs/pricing-tables. Fix the nav-detection over-fire (a nav is a SMALL header band of links, not a full-page repeated-row content list).
breadthFindings.note: Cloner is NOT overfit — does well on blog/product/ecommerce. responsive is the universal cap. The nav-misclassification + table-layout (structural=0) are the real generalization bugs.

structuredCloneDirection.direction: USER pointed at the deepest architectural lever: the build is a FLAT all-at-once pin, NOT the human-dev methodology (nav-first -> section-by-section -> blocks-within-sections, semantic containers + global styles). This flatness is the ROOT of: HN nav-misclassification (no nav-vs-section semantics), the responsive cap (no semantic section/block containers to reflow), shallow editability (flat pins not nesting). PLAN: research the methodology -> CLONE_METHODOLOGY.md -> restructure build into ordered hierarchical nav->section->block (build-structured.mjs). This is roadmap-tier (bigger than any single recipe) + serves the north star (clone the way a human dev would).

global-token extraction: cluster paint/typo -> Elementor Kit custom_colors/custom_typography; widgets reference globals not inline
DEPTA repeated-region -> native grid (card template xN in container_type:grid)
per-breakpoint container overrides (_tablet/_mobile flex_direction:column / grid->1) on semantic-container sections = TRUE reflow

MOBILE-MENU PATTERN FIDELITY: detect source mobile-nav pattern (hamburger / horizontal-scroll [Apple: nav-menu Horizontal+Pointer-None+Breakpoint-None] / app-footer) -> reproduce it per-source instead of always hamburger@390

segmentKeystoneV1.note: segment.mjs v1 built + design strong (separator/nav/footer/member rules all sound, NAVFIX reused, member coverage invariant). FAILS on 4/5: shallow partition (direct-children-of-content-root) emits 1 giant section when real bands nest deeper (tailwind real sections at depth 3 under h=11092 wrapper). v2 = recursive descent: split any band with h > ~0.5*pageH (or h > ~1.5*vw) that has >=2 children which are themselves distinct full-bleed/bg-bands; stop when coherent (bounded height OR children dont partition cleanly OR leaf-dense row). Keystone must hit >=80% sane before wiring into build-structured.mjs.

segmentV2.note: Recursive-descent VIPS works for nested-wrapper sites (tailwind 1->8, linear 1->11, supabase 12, no regression). Remaining failure mode = HEADING-LED same-bg content-width sections (basecamp sec1 = 63% block hiding 6 h2 sections) which full-bleed/bg recursion cannot split. FIX next: heading-led separator inside oversized same-bg bands, with a guard: split when there are FEW (~<=12) LARGE HETEROGENEOUS heading-blocks (basecamp); KEEP as ONE section when MANY (>~15) UNIFORM repeated items (overreacted blog list -> DEPTA #4). GATE LESSON (banked again): keystone-build rounds gate on STRICTLY-BETTER-THAN-PRIOR + no-regress + no-over-split, never an absolute sane-% floor (it auto-reverted a real win).

Both VALIDATE Joist: section-by-section copy/convert/import/fix; prompt discipline (ONLY Elementor widgets, use source colors/icons/fonts); error-feedback loop = our grade->refine; KEY (WPDev) bottleneck is DIRECTION not the plugin - vague prompt=generic page; Joist clone MEASURES geometry = inherently detailed direction. Bar in the wild is LOW (manual tweaking expected, clean only on Elementor-source sites). mcp-adapter = shared plumbing (validates arch). Security: live custom-code/scripts need scanning (motion escape-hatch).

interactionFidelityRequirement.note: USER raised the bar: 1:1 must include hover/dynamic/motion, not just pixels. Measured supabase.com live interaction vocab via Playwright getComputedStyle: 350 animated elements. DOMINANT: 131x color/bg/border/gradient transition @0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) (hover color shift on links/buttons/cards); 51x opacity@0.15s; 24x+7x transform/scale/translate@0.2s/0.15s (hover lift/scale); 15x full mega-transition (color+opacity+shadow+transform+filter+backdrop)@0.15s. KEYFRAMES: marquee@30-90s (logo strip + testimonial wall auto-scroll infinite), pulse@0.6s (accent dots), fadeIn@0.3s. LIBRARY: AOS (animate-on-scroll = scroll-reveal fade/translate-in). Buttons/CTA: 0.2s ease-out. GAP: grader measures NONE of this (static screenshots only) -> a clone with zero hover/marquee/scroll-reveal scores the same as one that nails them. PLAN: (1) capture = extend capture-layout with a hover+re-measure pass + record per-element transition/animation specs + AOS/scroll-trigger detection; (2) grade = NEW interaction-fidelity dimension (does clone reproduce source hover transitions + keyframe anims + scroll-reveals?) + add to eval-harness lived-experience probes; (3) build = emit Elementor native hover-state + transition-duration settings (links/buttons/cards = native) + route marquee/scroll-reveal/pulse to the custom-CSS/GSAP escape-hatch (validated by deep-research). Maps onto slices 1-8 + motion_vocabulary_artifacts.

Playwright-verified 8 matched landmark elements on supabase 12157: matched content near-perfect (text-color dE=0.00 on hero H1 / section H2 / feature H3 / body / footer link; Circular font loads+applies; sizes 72/36/16/14 match). BUT perelement-score.mjs L827-834 MULTIPLIES every sub-score by areaCoverage (0.3265) -> reported color a FALSE 0.2582. areaCoverage low only because 5 chunked-media bands (tweet wall ~56 runs, customer-logo strip, security band) are RASTERED -> ~107 source text/logo leaves never match -> dumped to unmatchedSrcArea. So the structured clone is MUCH closer to 1:1 than composite 0.576 says. matchRate 0.88. This is the false-deflation arm of grader_honesty_both_directions.

derasterNeedsRecapture.note: The 5 coverage-crushing rastered bands cannot be de-rastered from /tmp/glob-supa.json — capture flattened them to a single raster and dropped the child text/logo leaves. Until then, the coverage-crush + the grader false-deflation TOGETHER explain why the visually-good clone reads 0.576.

structuredGeneralizes.note: build-structured is a REAL GENERAL builder (not supabase-tuned). Fixable heuristic (detect link-row navs that are not button-kind), NOT architectural. RECOMMENDATION: wire build-structured into clone.mjs router (the reflow+editable option) + fix nav detection.

heldoutBreadthConfirms.note: build-structured GENERALIZES BROADLY (now 7 sites: supabase/tailwind/linear + held-out stripe/github/clerk/notion). REFLOW (0 horizontal scroll) held on ALL 7 - the user #2 fix is universal. nav held 5/7 (broke on linear + github = overlay/non-standard headers = the capture-side lever). graderBandMisalignment.note: SECOND grader false-deflation (distinct from the areaCoverage multiplier): grade-sections.mjs slices SOURCE-y bands and scores clone text/pixels per band, but the clone's content has drifted DOWN by hRatio (supa 2.01, stripe 1.22), so present-and-visible clone text (verified in DOM, cb-busted) is flagged missing-text/visual-degraded. FALSE POSITIVES. This PENALIZES the reflow/layout changes we want (any hRatio!=1 mis-bands). FIX (grader-side): map source bands to clone bands by content/anchor matching OR normalize by hRatio before banding; gate per-band editability on clone text in the CORRESPONDING clone band, not whole-page. Compounds the areaCoverage false-deflation. Verified: 40/40 supabase strings present+visible+0 occluded.

falseDeflationQuantified.note: GRADER FALSE-DEFLATION CONFIRMED + QUANTIFIED via flagged A/B (default provably untouched): the good supabase-structured clone scores composite 0.576 / pe 0.291 under the live grader but 0.649 / pe 0.709 un-conflated — the areaCoverage(0.337) multiply was falsely deflating a clone whose matched content is color-perfect (raw fidelity ~0.86). graderIsBindingConstraint.note: CONVERGED CONCLUSION: the BUILD is genuinely good (7-site reflow generalization, correct mobile grid-stacking [#35], right hero/nav/logos/globals, matched content color-perfect) but the GRADER is now the binding constraint on EVERY headline number. responsive stayed flat at 0.29 despite render-verified correct mobile reflow because the responsive term is dominated by edgeSet+coverage; per-element color reads 0.29 despite raw 0.79 because of the coverage multiply. globalsIllusion.note: CRITICAL BUG (corrects the recipe #32 win): the Kit global tokens are NOMINALLY referenced (__globals__ on ~91% of text widgets) but EFFECTIVELY INERT — build-structured emits text-editor widgets whose HTML carries inline style=color/font-family/font-size, which OVERRIDES the __globals__ var() by CSS specificity. So globalsCoverageEffective=0: flipping a Kit color/font token recolors NOTHING. The earlier globals round (#32/w4gtocg0g) verified the kit CSS var changed, not that the rendered widget recolored — the inline bake defeats it. FIX (autonomous-safe, grader-INDEPENDENTLY verifiable by token-flip-recolor): strip the inline color/font from text-editor HTML where a __globals__ ref + matching Kit token value exist, so the global wins. ALSO: editability grader FALSE-INFLATES (credits nominal __globals__ presence; should detect inline-override) -> add inline-override-effectiveness to grade-structure.mjs (supervised grader item). The grader lies BOTH ways: false-DEFLATES visual (coverage multiply) + false-INFLATES editability (nominal globals).

Editable literally but no headline/body distinction + no native widget controls. autonomous fix (HEURISTIC, supervised-flagged for semantic/SEO risk): promote text-editor->heading (large/top-of-section), ->button, ->image native widgets (deterministic, asset-preserving) — review on a sample first. Plus low-risk hygiene: unwrap 14/12 redundant single-child wrappers + auto-name sections (_title) for the Navigator.

REFLOW_NOT_MISSING_LOCALIZED.note: grade-spec.mjs (NEW per-section grader) de-blurred the supabase good-clone composite: the build-structured clone's gap is LAYOUT REFLOW, NOT missing content. Per-section positional coverage mean 0.093, weakest hero/logos/features. Content present (text matches) but collapsed to a left-aligned single column (src centered/3-col -> clone left/1-col) with non-uniform vertical proportions. This is BY DESIGN of build-structured (the reflow builder trades positional 1:1 for responsive+editable) — but it's exactly why the user said the clone 'doesn't feel close / width not adjusting'. ACTIONABLE: (a) decompose gap into reflow-vs-missing via a per-band-anchored content-present grade (grade-spec v2); (b) then fix build-structured layout fidelity (preserve centered content column max-width + multi-col grids at desktop width) so the reflowed clone still feels close. CLUSTERROWS_COLLAPSE_ROOTCAUSE.note: THE supabase 'doesn't feel close' root cause, LOCALIZED + live-DOM-verified (round wsqygr33h): build-structured clusterRows()/rowColumns() (L509-542) collapse dense MIXED-SIZE feature grids to a single full-width column. Mechanism: (a) clusterRows row-gap = max(12, medianMemberHeight*0.5) — tall media cards (h~398) inflate the median so the gap balloons (~199px) and the entire multi-row card grid chains into ONE y-row; (b) rowColumns merges members whose x-intervals overlap within 1.2%*sectionW — wide media cards overlap the narrow text columns AND each other, collapsing N columns to 1. With cols=1, RAM-grid (#35) never qualifies (needs >=2 comparable cells) so the section becomes a tall vertical stack. This doubles page height (heightRatio 2.05) and is the direct cause of the user's 'width not adjusting / doesn't feel close' feedback. FIX DIRECTION (the section-spec layer's payoff): for spec role in {features,pricing,gallery,logos,testimonial} (multi-cell), recover the grid robustly by clustering columns from the NARROW heading/text element x-centers (ignoring wide spanning media for column derivation), and make clusterRows row-gap robust to tall outliers (cap it, don't let one tall card inflate it). Gate strictly: flag-off byte-identical, flag-on fixes #2 grid + drops heightRatio toward ~1, corpus no-h-scroll no-regression, codex-verify.

CODEX_INTERMITTENT.note: Codex availability is INTERMITTENT in this env: it answered the probe + verified section-spec + per-section-grade earlier today, then ~20min later FAILED again with the gpt-5.3-codex/gpt-4o ChatGPT-account model-compat error. So treat codex as BEST-EFFORT (policy already says so): try it; if it errors, fall back to a fresh independent same-model (Claude) reviewer (which VERIFIED the GRIDFIX cleanly). Do NOT block a default-OFF, deterministically-proven core-build change on codex being up.

GRIDFIX_NEXT.note: After GRIDFIX (dense-grid collapse fixed, heightRatio 2.05->1.52), the RANKED next levers to push supabase toward 1:1: (1) grade-spec v2 = per-band y-ANCHOR (align each band top, scale within-band) so the grader fairly credits non-uniformly-reflowed-but-present content (v1 single-y-scale undercredits — it scored the improved build only 0.145 despite the real structural gain); (2) SECONDARY height defects keeping heightRatio at 1.52: testimonial/tweet walls #9/#10 stretch + the centered-column-width defect (hero/section text emits width:100% of the boxed inner x=150 w=1140 instead of the source's tight ~604px centered column -> looks left-heavy + taller); (3) validate GRIDFIX across the corpus render-side then consider default-ON. The user's 'doesn't feel close' is now partly addressed (grids restored) + precisely measurable.

BENTO_OVERLAP_FLEX_LIMIT.note: FUNDAMENTAL LIMIT (honest hard truth) surfaced by the clean per-section height diag: build-structured (flex/grid reflow) CANNOT replicate the source's BENTO/OVERLAP-PACKED image sections. supabase #2 packs ten ~400px product images into a 1048px band via overlap/layering; #8 clips a 620px image into a 261px band. Flex cannot overlap -> it stacks -> clone sections render 3x tall (the dominant remaining heightRatio 1.52 contributor; ~2800px of the ~3968px excess is #2+#8). The images are at TRUE captured size (not a cap bug). FIXES are all trade-offs: (a) stack tall [current, faithful-content/wrong-height]; (b) scale images down [wrong-size/distorts]; (c) drop images [loses content]; (d) ABSOLUTE/overlap positioning [build-absolute territory — positional 1:1 but loses reflow]. The principled answer = a HYBRID/ROUTER that uses absolute-overlap for bento-heavy sections + flex for the rest — an ARCHITECTURAL reflow-vs-positional decision (user has weighed this trade-off before; surface it, don't autonomously hack a distorting heuristic). build-structured is CORRECT for clean column/grid sections (hero/logos/cta/standard features all 1.0-1.4x faithful).

GRIDFIX_GENERALIZES.note: GRIDFIX generalizes (offline corpus A/B, flag-off vs flag-on, grid-recovery + no-h-scroll + valid-tree): supabase 3->7 grids, tailwind 2->5, vercel 0->3, linear 2->3 (recovers collapsed grids on 4/6), basecamp 7->7 (neutral), overreacted 0->0 (correct no-op on a blog list). ALL pass selftest flag-on (no h-scroll regression); flag-off byte-identical everywhere. => STRONG DEFAULT-ON CANDIDATE. Caveat before flipping the default: offline proves structure+no-scroll on all 6; render-side heightRatio is render-proven only on supabase (2.05->1.52). Pre-default-on step = per-site publish+capture heightRatio A/B (flag-off vs on) to confirm the grid recovery reduces (or is neutral on) rendered height per site. NOT flipped autonomously (core-builder default = supervised + deserves per-site render proof).

GRADESPEC_COALESCING_DEFLATION.note: grade-spec --anchored UNDER-measures clones that COALESCE leaves: tailwind clone has 71 leaves vs 142 src (build-structured merges multi-leaf text into single text widgets), so per-leaf anchor matching only credits ~1 of each merged group -> 0.054 on a structurally-sound 1.12-heightRatio clone. The per-section CONTENT-presence metric is therefore untrustworthy on coalesced sites (height-attribution via segment.mjs is the reliable structural metric, but does NOT measure content presence). FIX (offline, additive, reversible): coalescing-aware ONE-TO-MANY matching — a single clone leaf may satisfy MULTIPLE src leaves IF it spatially covers their scaled positions AND its text CONTAINS their tokens (so a coalesced clone paragraph credits all the src lines it absorbs). ANTI-GAMING preserved: a clone leaf can only claim src leaves whose CONTENT it actually subsumes -> a clone missing content still cannot claim it (self-test: identity stays 1.0, incomplete-clone stays low). Same honesty-fix CLASS as the prior areaCoverage/single-y-scale false-deflations the user flagged.

BENTO_CONFIRMED_CROSS_SITE.note: BENTO/OVERLAP confirmed as THE consistent remaining 1:1 frontier across BOTH probed sites (supabase #2/#8, tailwind #2). Irregular bento (side-by-side panels/mockups of varying size packed via overlap) is the one class GRIDFIX can't recover (it assumes a regular N-col grid with >=2 narrow cells/col; a bento has irregular cells + single large elements per cell). Everything else generalizes near-1:1. This is the highest-leverage remaining target BUT architectural (flex can't overlap; absolute breaks the no-h-scroll invariant the user requires) -> needs the reflow-vs-positional hybrid decision (user). GRIDFIX render-proven on 2 sites now (supabase 2.05->1.52, tailwind 1.12) => default-on candidate pending render-check on the sites where it changes output (basecamp/vercel/linear).

GRADESPEC_MATURE.note: grade-spec is now MATURE/trustworthy: identity=1.0; anti-gaming proven 3 ways (incomplete 0.252, gamed-headings 0.275, garbage-token mega 0.328<0.494); credits reflow (v2 anchored) + coalescing (one-to-many subsumption); cleanly SEPARATES content-presence (textCoverage) from media/positional fidelity (area-coverage). The NEW signal it surfaces: MEDIA POSITIONAL FIDELITY — sections with high textCoverage but low area-coverage = the clone's images/mockups are present-as-content but not at the source size/position (distinct from missing content). This + bento are the two remaining 1:1 frontiers. Use the segment-based height-attribution as the reliable structural metric + grade-spec anchored (content) + area (media) as the per-section fidelity split.

COLWIDTH_HEIGHTSAFE_NEXT.note: STRUCT_COLWIDTH (per-section source content-column width + alignment) is net-beneficial (exact horizontal match + grade-spec area-coverage +0.089) and one tweak from keepable: only the HERO regressed height (+1.2% whole-page) by wrapping its heading to 2 lines in the narrowed column (clone font wider than source's Circular at the same width). Sections 3/5/7/8/11 narrowed with ZERO height change. FIX (v2 height-safe): apply colwidth per-section but skip/clamp any section whose narrowing increases its rendered height (the hero) — keep area-coverage gain on the safe sections, zero height regression. SEPARATE deeper lever surfaced: FONT-WIDTH fidelity — 29/126 leaves fall back to sans-serif (not the source Circular), and uppercase/letter-spacing deltas make headings wider than source -> contributes to wrap + visual mismatch. Worth a later font-fidelity round (register/serve ALL source font weights; honor exact letter-spacing/transform).

SESSION_STACK_STATE.note: Current best supabase clone (12157, flags GRIDFIX+COLWIDTH on): heightRatio 1.523, grade-spec anchored area 0.456, NO h-scroll, native+editable, grids recovered, source content-width on safe sections, correct Circular font on 97/126 leaves. SESSION WINS (all verified, all default-OFF behind flags so default build is unchanged): section-spec layer; grade-spec v1/v2-anchored/coalescing (mature trustworthy grader); STRUCT_GRIDFIX (dense-grid collapse fix, generalizes 4/6 corpus); STRUCT_COLWIDTH (source content-width, +22% area). (2) BENTO/overlap (#2/#8) = the dominant residual height, ARCHITECTURAL (reflow-vs-positional, user's call). (4) media-positional fidelity (likely overlaps bento).

LINKLIST_COLUMNS_DEFECT.note: NEW tractable defect class (discovered via 3rd-site generalization on basecamp): LINK-LIST / CSS-COLUMNS <ul> COLLAPSE. A long list of bare <a> anchors that the SOURCE lays out in N short CSS columns (footer sitemap / 'And there's more' index / mega-menu) gets STACKED 1-anchor-per-row into ONE tall full-width column by build-structured (all 34 basecamp footer links at x=190 in the clone). RAM-grid (#35) only fires on card/grid cells of comparable width, never on bare-anchor link lists, so there is no multi-column-list recipe. DOMINANT basecamp height inflator (footer 492->1235px, +743px, ratio 2.51). DISTINCT from bento/overlap, grid-cells-tall, font/wrap. FIX (tractable, generalizes — footer sitemaps are universal): detect a link-list container (>=~8 short bare anchors, low non-anchor text, narrow rows) and emit it as a CSS multi-column block (column-width ~= source link-column width, or column-count from the source x-distribution) so it flows into the source's N columns instead of stacking. Gate like GRIDFIX: default-off, byte-identical-off, heightRatio down on basecamp, no h-scroll, corpus no-reg, verify.

DEFAULTON_DERISKED.note: GRIDFIX+COLWIDTH default-on is now render-validated on 3 DIVERSE sites with NO regression: supabase (heightRatio 2.05->1.52, grids+colwidth help), tailwind (1.12), basecamp (1.351, the GRIDFIX 'neutral 7->7' edge case — 5/7 sections 1.02-1.07, hero strip recovered, no h-scroll). + offline-validated on 6 + geometrically sound (grid<=stack) + independently verified + revert flags (STRUCT_NO_GRIDFIX / unset STRUCT_COLWIDTH). The flip is FULLY de-risked; left default-off only because I deferred the product-default decision to the user. Recommend: flip both default-on (with revert flags) when the user confirms — or it's safe to flip autonomously if the mandate prefers shipping.

DARK_BG_CAPTURE_DEFECT.note: NEW dominant defect (vercel, 4th-site discovery): DARK/GRADIENT BACKGROUND FIDELITY LOSS. Dark sections (prism hero panel, dark CTA, footer) + canvas/gradient hero render FLAT WHITE in the clone. ROOT CAUSE = CAPTURE-side: segment(vercel-src) reports all 7 bands as light rgb(248-250) — the band-bg sampler reads the band node's CSS background-color (the light wrapper) and MISSES (a) a dark full-bleed INNER panel that paints the section dark, and (b) canvas/WebGL/complex-gradient fills. So build-structured has no dark bg to propagate. FIX (capture-side, capture-layout.mjs, foundational/risky): sample each band's TRUE rendered background from the captured screenshot pixels in the band's NON-content region (or walk children to find a dark full-bleed painter), so dark sections carry their real dark color; region-capture canvas/gradient heroes as images (harder, later). CRITICAL GATE: re-capturing the 3 light proven sites (supabase/tailwind/basecamp) must keep their correct LIGHT band bgs (no capture regression to the proven clones). Valuable class: dark-mode/dev-tool/modern-design sites (2026 bias). ALSO re-prioritized: nav mega-menu over-capture (hover-hidden dropdown subtree flattened into 804px of visible links) = capture-side overlay/hidden-content fix (collapse display:none/hover-hidden subtrees to the trigger row) — the supervisedQueue 'capture-overlay-headers' item.

HEADLESS_RENDER_FIDELITY.note: DEEPER capture-environment gap surfaced by the darkbg round: vercel.com renders ENTIRELY LIGHT in this headless capture (document colorScheme=light, body rgb(250), ZERO <canvas>, prism hero = pastel CSS gradient, 0 dark page-rows even with --use-angle=swiftshader GPU) — whereas vercel's real-browser design is dark. Same family as the Stripe 'doesn't render headless' outlier. CAUSE candidates: prefers-color-scheme defaulting to light in headless (real users/site default may be dark), JS-gated theming not running, or bot/variant serving. IMPACT: the capture is faithful to HEADLESS but headless != what a real user sees for some sites -> the clone of such sites is 'correct vs headless' but wrong vs the real design. FIXES (capture-environment, later/user): emulate prefers-color-scheme:dark (Playwright colorScheme) when the site's default is dark; ensure full JS execution + settle; consider a headed/real-browser capture path for JS/canvas-heavy or theme-switching sites (framer/Stripe class). NOT a build/grader bug — the pipeline faithfully reproduces the (light) headless render.

DARKBG_FEATURE_READY.note: CAPTURE_BANDBG (rendered band-bg gutter-sampling, default-OFF) is BUILT + framer-proven + supabase-no-regression + no-h-scroll + has an anti-false-darkening guard (only adopts a sampled bg when isDarkOrColored: avg<110 OR avg<=230 with chroma>=60; sets BOTH node.bgSampled AND node.background.color so it surfaces over the light wrapper CSS). It was reverted only due to the wrong (now-light) test site (vercel). RE-GATE on framer (genuinely dark in headless, 3 dark sections) + supabase-no-regression to ADOPT it default-off — a real fidelity capability for the dark-mode/dev/modern site class. Insertion point: capture-layout.mjs right after modalBg/sampleBg; segment.mjs bandBg reads bgSampled (L113) but prefers background.color (L108) so the feature sets both.

SCHEMA_AGENT_PLUMBING.note: Workflow schema-agents have now failed twice with 'subagent completed without calling StructuredOutput after 2 nudges' (wpagss0i1 4-stream design; wir2f8f6i darkbg-v2). Both were capture/long-running agents. MITIGATION: for long/capture-heavy agents, return TEXT (no schema) + parse a VERDICT line in the workflow JS, OR keep schemas only on short deterministic agents. The schema rounds that SUCCEEDED (grade-spec, gridfix, colwidth, linkcols, generalization) were also capture-heavy though, so it's intermittent — but TEXT-return is the robust dodge when it recurs. ALWAYS back up + auto-restore foundational files (capture-layout) since a mid-flight schema-fail leaves them modified (caught + restored here).

HEADLESS_RENDER_IS_THE_FRONTIER.note: CONVERGED INSIGHT: the remaining hard defects are ONE umbrella issue — HEADLESS-RENDER FIDELITY. The headless capture does not match what a real browser shows for dynamic/themed/interactive sites: (1) vercel renders LIGHT in headless (real = dark); (2) mega-menu dropdowns are captured OPEN (real = closed-until-hover) -> the 804px nav; (3) canvas/WebGL/complex-gradient bgs missed; (4) Stripe renders BLANK headless. Build/segment/filter fixes FIGHT THE SYMPTOM and mismatch the (wrong) headless grader — proven: CAPTURE_VISIBLE over-filtered real content (show-more walls/marquees use the same opacity/visibility/overflow CSS). The REAL lever = capture-ENVIRONMENT: (a) emulate prefers-color-scheme to match the site default; (b) longer JS-settle + network-idle; (c) reset interaction state (no hover, scroll-to-top) before measuring; (d) a HEADED/real-browser capture path for canvas/JS/theme-heavy sites (framer/Stripe/vercel class). This is DEEP + somewhat architectural (capture-environment redesign) -> best as a careful dedicated effort / with user input, NOT blunt autonomous filtering. The build-structured EASY-WIN flywheel is complete (9 verified recipes/graders); the frontier is now capture-environment + bento-architecture (both deeper).

PERSITE_COLORSCHEME_FIX.note: TRACTABLE headless-render fix found (diagnosis wm1p5l9y5): emulate each site's DECLARED preferred color-scheme before capture. Mechanism: read meta[name=color-scheme] first token (vercel='dark light'->dark) or :root computed color-scheme; if dark-preferred, page.emulateMedia({colorScheme:'dark'}) + settle, then capture -> the page renders its TRUE dark design (real CSS dark bgs, no gutter-sampling needed). PROVEN-safe per-site: vercel->dark (correct), supabase->light (unchanged, 0 dark), framer->already-dark-intrinsic (unaffected). A GLOBAL colorScheme:dark is UNSAFE (darkens light sites) — must be PER-SITE-DECLARED. This is the genuine capture-env win for the dark-default-site class (2026 design bias). Gate: vercel dark>=2 + supabase light(0) + framer dark>=3 + proven-sites no-regression; default-off flag (CAPTURE_COLORSCHEME).

GRADESPEC_COLOR_BLIND.note: GRADER GAP (found validating the dark fixes): grade-spec.mjs scores ONLY structural/textual band coverage (matched src-leaf area + text chars, anchored per section) — it has ZERO background/color/contrast dimension. So a clone that renders a dark site ENTIRELY WHITE scores IDENTICALLY to a correct dark clone (both anchoredMean 0.345). This is why the validated dark fixes (vercel 0->7 dark bands, visually dark) did not move the metric — the grader is orthogonal to color. FIX (grader-completeness, the user's 'optimize grader honesty' value): add a per-section BACKGROUND/COLOR-fidelity dimension to grade-spec (src band bg vs clone band bg deltaE / dark-light match; optionally dominant text color) folded into the score or a colorMatch sub-metric. Gate: identity 1.0 (src-vs-src color perfect); ANTI-GAMING (a wrong-color clone scores low); the dark vercel clone (7/7 dark vs dark src) scores HIGH while the white baseline (0 dark vs dark src) scores LOW -> proves it credits the dark fix + penalizes white; supabase (light/light) ~unchanged. This is the natural next grader improvement — it credits the just-validated dark work + catches color mismatch everywhere.

SECTION_BAND_MISALIGN.note: NEXT structural defect (vercel, post-dark-fix): SECTION-BAND MISALIGNMENT + uneven vertical stretch — content is present (src 220 / clone 186 leaves, comparable) but the section bands are mis-aligned / unevenly stretched between src and clone. Distinct from bento/font/link-list. Candidate next structural lever after the grader color dimension.

AUTONOMOUS_TRACTABLE_EXHAUSTED.note: The autonomously-tractable build/capture/grader levers are now largely EXHAUSTED. BANKED (11 verified, all default-off + revert-flagged): section-spec layer; grade-spec v1/v2-anchored/coalescing/COLOR (mature 5-dim grader); STRUCT_GRIDFIX (dense-grid collapse); STRUCT_COLWIDTH (source content-width); STRUCT_LINKCOLS (link-list CSS-columns); CAPTURE_BANDBG (dark band sampling); CAPTURE_COLORSCHEME (per-site dark-default render). Generalized 4 archetypes (heightRatio 1.12-1.56). REMAINING RESIDUALS are all ARCHITECTURAL or USER-DEFERRED: (1) BENTO/overlap (supabase #2/#8/#6, vercel #2 = the dominant residual) — flex can't overlap-pack; absolute fix reintroduces h-scroll (user's hard-no) -> USER's reflow-vs-positional decision; (2) DEFAULT-ON flip of the 5 recipes (render-validated, revert-flagged) -> USER's product-default decision (deferred per stated bar, now met); (3) Stripe-blank / headed-capture (deep capture-env); (4) dense pricing/comparison TABLES (recipe #36 lost <table> semantics — a KNOWN tractable weak spot, the current discovery target). Forward autonomous progress now = DISCOVERY on new page-types/archetypes (the pattern that yielded GRIDFIX/LINKCOLS/COLORSCHEME) + the table fix; deeper levers need the user.

WRITES_PERSIST_VERIFIED.note: INTEGRITY CHECK PASSED: live https://georges232.sg-host.com/structured-supabase/ (page 12157) carries 7 ramgrid + 4 colw + 4 linkcols custom_css markers -> the full-stack build PERSISTED. So the 422 atomic_save_silent_failure on Elementor 4.0.9 is HARMLESS (postmeta/_elementor_data propagates), as long established. ALL session render-side measurements (GRIDFIX 2.05->1.52, colwidth, linkcols, heightRatios) are VALID. A subagent's 'Elementor 4.0.9 refuses every write' claim was a mis-diagnosis from capturing a stale 12446 scratch page. TABLES_CONFIRMED_WORKING.note: Recipe #36 (native semantic tables) CONFIRMED WORKING end-to-end on a dense pricing comparison matrix: captured as <table> -> matched -> built faithful 4-col table with row-headers/colgroup/td preserved. The earlier 'dense matrix lost <table> semantics' (multi-page round) is FIXED. Tables are NOT a remaining defect.

BENTO_IS_TILE_GRID.note: SUPERSEDES BENTO_OVERLAP_FLEX_LIMIT (partially): the dominant bento residual (supabase #2) is a REGULAR 4-col x 2-row tile grid (heading-x column anchors 211/494/777/1060 @283px pitch; heading-y row anchors 832/1241; one col-span-2 Postgres tile; members snap within 13px). The 3.08x stretch is side-by-side-vs-stack ONLY (flex stacks 7 tall tiles -> 2800px vs grid 2 rows -> 855px). NO true cross-tile overlap. FIX (tractable, autonomous, respects no-h-scroll): a TILE-BENTO recipe — detect a tile-bento (>=4 heading-led tiles on N column-anchors x M row-anchors), GROUP members into tiles (heading + nearby text/image), emit a CSS GRID (reuse the RAM-grid container_type:grid kses-safe channel) with N columns + tile placement (incl col-span for double-wide). Recovers ~855px vs 2800px. GRIDFIX gridded #2's columns but tiles still stacked (it groups single cells, not multi-element TILES) -> the tile-grouping + 2-row placement is the gap. Gate: supabase #2 hRatio 3.08->~1-1.3 + no-h-scroll + flag-off byte-identical + corpus no-reg. NOTE: truly-irregular/overlapping bentos (if any) still need the user arch-call, but the dominant supabase case is regular -> autonomously fixable.

CARDWALL_DETECTOR_NEXT.note: NEXT lever (the new dominant residual after imgfit): heading-less MASONRY card-wall sections (supabase testimonials #4/#9/#10 ~52% of remaining overage). Structure (diagnosed): many comparable-width CARDS at distinct x-column anchors (#9: 5 cols x=46/366/686/1006/1326, ~254px each, UNEVEN heights = masonry; #10: 4-col; #4: 4 mockup customer-story cards). RAMGRID misses them (wants comparable-HEIGHT cells), BENTOGRID misses them (needs >=4 HEADINGS; these are heading-less avatar+handle+tweet cards). FIX: a card-wall detector — detect a section dominated by >=6 comparable-WIDTH cards at >=3 x-column anchors (heading-less OK), EXCLUDE any full-bleed backdrop (e.g. #9's 1400x524 mockup ~ its band, must NOT be gridded), emit as CSS multi-column (column-width = card pitch; handles UNEVEN masonry heights better than grid) or auto-fit grid -> cards pack into N columns instead of stacking. kses-safe/no-h-scroll. Gate: #9/#10/#4 hRatio down + backdrop preserved + byte-identical-off + corpus no-reg.

CARDWALL_WORKS_REGATE.note: STRUCT_CARDWALL (heading-less masonry card-wall -> CSS columns) WORKS (supabase #9 1.60->1.09, #10 1.46->1.25, no-h-scroll, no corpus false-pos, byte-identical-off) but was reverted by an OVER-STRICT gate that demanded #4 too. #4 is NOT a card-wall (irregular image-mosaic, gcv 0.47 ~ tailwind #2 0.40 -> can't admit without a false-positive); the pitch-regularity guard rightly excludes it. BACKDROP FIX: #9's full-bleed backdrop must render via the scoped custom_css background-image (#cardwall-N{background-image:url;background-size:cover}) NOT the Elementor section background_image setting (kses strips bg-IMAGES on this 4.0.9 stack; bg-COLOR survives, but image needs the custom_css channel). Re-running.

ENGINE_GENERALIZES_NEAR_1TO1.note: SESSION THESIS VALIDATED: the build-structured engine + 8 recipes (GRIDFIX/COLWIDTH/LINKCOLS/BENTOGRID/IMGFIT/CARDWALL build; CAPTURE_BANDBG/COLORSCHEME capture) + the mature 5-dim grade-spec (structural+text+coalescing+anchored+color) clones DIVERSE archetypes to NEAR-1:1 with ZERO cross-site regression, NO horizontal scroll, native+editable, all default-OFF + revert-flagged. heightRatios: supabase 1.142 (from 2.05 collapse), tailwind 1.129, basecamp 1.209. CONTENT_COMPLETENESS_SOLID.note: Content-completeness VERIFIED solid: basecamp clone preserves 100% of source BODY text (69/69) despite a lower leaf count — the build COALESCES tiny source leaves into fewer widgets (same words). The raw leaf-count / per-leaf coverage UNDER-reads coalesced clones; the coalescing-aware grade-spec is the right metric. No content-loss defect anywhere found. (Reminder: nav is built only on --publish, absent from --dry dumps — not a loss.)

GRADER_TRANSPARENT_BG_QUIRK.note: grade-spec colorMatch FALSE-NEGATIVE: a TRANSPARENT source background (rgba(0,0,0,0) / transparent / null) is parsed by parseRgb as literal BLACK rgb(0,0,0). When a section has bg:default it falls back to the page bg; if the source page bg is transparent, it reads black, and the clone's (white) page bg yields a spurious ~0.013 colorMatch on every default-bg section. FIX (small, grader-honesty): treat transparent/null/none bg as the EFFECTIVE rendered default (page-default light/white rgb(255,255,255)) before colorMatch — OR skip colorMatch for default/transparent-bg sections (they inherit the page, so match by page bg which should itself default transparent->white). Gate: --selftest + --color-selftest still pass (identity 1.0, anti-gaming); tailwind transparent-bg sections -> colorMatch ~1.0 (light) not 0.013; vercel/framer dark (REAL dark) UNAFFECTED; supabase (light) unaffected. The dark fixes themselves are CONFIRMED generalizing (real dark bands score 1.0).

HEADED_RESCUES_CANVAS.note: CAPTURE_HEADED (default-off) — TRUE-HEADED browser launch WORKS in this sandbox (has a display) and RESCUES canvas/WebGL rasters that plain headless blank-drops: Stripe 0->6 non-blank canvas rasters (gradient hero 1393x761 + surfaces). CORRECTION to prior 'Stripe blank in headless': Stripe renders FINE headless here (316 leaves) — the gap is CANVAS/WebGL gradient/animated heroes, not leaf-blank. The headed feature reverted only because the gate measured leaf-count (no change) not canvas-raster-count (0->6 = the real win). VALUE: canvas/gradient/animated-hero fidelity for modern/Awwwards-tier sites (the user's motion interest). RE-GATE on canvas-raster rescue + flag-off-unchanged + light-no-regression.

RESPONSIVE_CONFIRMED.note: Engine RESPONSIVE-validated: the default near-1:1 clone has NO visible horizontal scroll at desktop/tablet/mobile (documentElement.scrollWidth==viewport) + sane reflow (RAM-grid auto-fit collapses N->1, colwidth max-width:100% shrinks, sections stack). User complaint #2 (horizontal-scroll) is robustly solved across viewports. MINOR latent nit (future polish, low-value): a ~28px element overflow at 390 is MASKED by overflow-x:hidden (no visible scroll; safety-net works) — fixing the underlying element (find the >viewport-width member at 390, constrain max-width:100%) would be true correctness but the symptom is already handled. RLG headline 0.31 is SOURCE-DRIFT (live supabase.com is a different/taller page now), not a clone defect.

RESPONSIVE_FULLY_CLEAN.note: Responsive now FULLY clean (not just masked): the only mobile overflow (supabase #2 bento span-2 track, +48px at 390) is fixed at the source via a span-aware grid-track floor (calc((100%-(maxSpan-1)*gap)/maxSpan)) — N spanned tracks + gaps fit at any width, no reliance on overflow-x:hidden. Generalizes to any spanned bento/grid. Engine: no visible AND no actual h-scroll at 1440/768/390.

MOTION_DIMENSION_OPENED.note: Motion fidelity is now MEASURABLE (grade-motion.mjs shadow). Captures scroll-reveal/parallax/pin + hover deltas + motion-library fingerprint; richness-weighted source-vs-clone agreement; selftest 1.0 (deepcopy) deterministic; discriminates animated(supabase 0.12)-vs-static. GSAP escape-hatch + marquee keyframes = later heavier rounds.

The capture->grade->build->regrade-climb loop is closed: grade-motion --dump captures source hover profile -> STRUCT_MOTION emits native Elementor hover (kses-safe, no JS) reproducing it -> live grade-motion CLIMBS (0.2341->0.2695, hover 57->68/78, prop-set 1.0). REMAINING motion gaps (next inches, by leverage): SCROLL-REVEAL entrance animations (most COMMON pattern, native Elementor `animation` setting, kses-safe; static-render risk needs hard composite-no-reg gate since entrance hides content until revealed -- capture commitStyles recipe #96 should finish them); MARQUEE (biggest single supabase count 23, CSS keyframe via custom_css); GSAP escape-hatch (what native can't do); THEN wire grade-motion into the composite weight (justified once builder climbs it).

MOTION_LOOP_REVEAL_DONE.note: Motion reproduction now covers HOVER + SCROLL-REVEAL, both native Elementor, both default-off opt-in, both reversible + faithful + zero static regression. KEY PROOF: entrance animations (which hide content until revealed) do NOT regress the composite because capture-layout recipe-#96 reveal-pass finishes them to opacity:1 before capture. REMAINING reproduction inches (by leverage): MARQUEE (supabase biggest single signal ~23; CSS @keyframes translateX loop via scoped custom_css; RISKS to gate hard: kses @keyframes survival, infinite-loop at-rest capture, h-scroll from translate -> need overflow:hidden + no-h-scroll gate); PARALLAX (supabase ~5, may be partly noise); GSAP escape-hatch (for what native cannot do). CONSOLIDATION after primitives: (a) wire motion into the DEFAULT clone pipeline (clone.mjs auto-dump signals + apply hover+reveal) so motion ships as default not opt-in; (b) THEN wire grade-motion into the composite weight (justified once default clones carry motion to be graded on).

BUILDER_TOPOLOGY_MOTION_INTEGRATION.note: DISCOVERY (gates the motion ship-it step): clone.mjs (product entry: capture-ensemble -> build -> grade) uses build-ABSOLUTE / build-hybrid / build-sectionraster. But ALL the STRUCT_* recipes + the new motion (STRUCT_MOTION hover, STRUCT_MOTION_REVEAL) live in build-STRUCTURED.mjs (the responsive reflow builder + the grade-structure/grade-spec corpus path). So motion is currently proven+opt-in in build-structured, NOT in the clone.mjs product path. This + wiring grade-motion into the COMPOSITE (changes the headline objective) are ARCHITECTURAL/product decisions worth surfacing to the user rather than deciding autonomously. Meanwhile, disciplined autonomous continuation = keep adding reproduction primitives WITHIN build-structured (marquee next), all gated+reversible.
userDecisionWorthy: wire grade-motion into composite objective (deflates headline until motion coverage full)
builder unification (absolute pixel-1:1 vs structured responsive-reflow)

MARQUEE_ROOT_CAUSE_HEIGHT_INFLATION.note: Marquee v1 (wnrmonfp2) failed ONLY gate5 (composite), and the cause is a BUILD-side defect, NOT a grader artifact: the inline 2x member duplication (for a seamless loop) inflated section height (hRatio 1.09->1.25, heightPenalty 1.0->0.908) -> visual 0.854->0.767 (-0.087, stable across 3 runs: 0.766/0.767/0.767); editability 0.899 unchanged, designSystem actually up 0.909->0.918. WHY: the structured builders same-y row-clustering wrapped the DOUBLED members into MULTIPLE rows instead of one horizontal strip. All OTHER gates PASSED (kses @keyframes survived + 4 tracks animate live; no-h-scroll @1440/768/390 via overflow:hidden clip; byte-id-off; faithful; selftest 1.0). FIX (confident, build-side): force the marquee TRACK to a SINGLE non-wrapping horizontal row (display:flex; flex-wrap:nowrap; width:max-content; white-space:nowrap) so the 2x duplicate extends SIDEWAYS (clipped), adding ZERO height; prevent row-clustering from re-clustering the marquee members. DECISIVE re-gate: marquee-clone section height == no-marquee height (hRatio/heightPenalty unchanged) -> visual no-reg. -> marquee-v2.

After cross-site validation, the remaining levers are heavy/user-decision -> natural pause-for-direction point.

MOTION_GENERALIZES_EXCEPT_LABOKLCH.note: Capstone proved motion GENERALIZES across the corpus (reveal, marquee-absence, faithfulness, byte-id-off, no-h-scroll all clean on tailwind/basecamp/vercel; tailwind LIVE zero composite regression; per-site profiles genuinely differ). Gate: re-run generalization -> tailwind+basecamp hover injects (>0) + live kses-survives + vercel unchanged + byte-id-off + selftest 1.0.

finding_linear_dark_headings: IMPORTANT: linear dark-on-dark headings are a SEPARATE bug from resend gradient-text (fix #1 did NOT cover them). The earlier CASE-B stale-page theory is DISPROVEN -- linear rebuilt 3x since (fix#1/#2/#3 all targeted it) yet headings STILL dark-on-dark. So linear headings use a different color mechanism (CSS-var / different clip / wrong sampled color) that capture records as dark. = next high-leverage fix (6 tiles).
headingRootCause: RESOLVED ROOT: resend gradient-text heading DETECTION is non-deterministic in capture-layout (glyph-pixel sampling of transparent-fill text). When capture classifies kind=gradient-text -> hardened build fix -> white (linear: classified -> headings recovered, +8.6). When capture FAILS to classify (samples the transparent/black fill) -> heading falls to the normal-text path -> black title_color. NOT a CDN cache issue (plain==bust==black now) and NOT the build (build emits white when fed gradient-text; step-4 probe was white). The real fix = make capture gradient-text detection DETERMINISTIC: key on DOM CSS (background-clip:text|webkit-background-clip:text + -webkit-text-fill-color:transparent + a background-image gradient) NOT on glyph-pixel sampling.
